en.title: Website prospecting: qualify companies from their sites
en.seoTitle: Website Prospecting Guide for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
en.description: Use website prospecting to qualify prospects, spot source-backed outreach angles, and decide which companies deserve personal outreach.
en.intent: Explain the category for individual operators searching for a better way to prospect from websites.
heroBullets: Start from a real company website instead of a generic contact list.
Score fit with visible evidence: CTAs, proof, positioning, content gaps, and contact paths.
Turn each observation into a first line, outreach angle, and export-ready note.

sections.title: What website prospecting means
sections.copy: Website prospecting is the process of qualifying a company by reading the public signals on its site before you write outreach. For solo professionals, the website often contains the useful angle: unclear positioning, weak proof, hidden calls to action, stale content, missing service pages, or conversion gaps.
items: It is useful when your offer improves websites, SEO, conversion, demand generation, or messaging.
It creates a reason to contact the prospect that is visible and source-backed.
It keeps you from saving leads just because a company matches an industry filter.

sections.title: Signals worth capturing
sections.copy: The best signals are not trivia. They connect a page observation to a business outcome the prospect already cares about. A weak CTA matters because it can reduce demo intent. A missing case study matters because buyers may need proof before booking.
items: Above-the-fold clarity: headline, offer, CTA, and proof placement.
Trust proof: case studies, testimonials, logos, regulated-industry evidence, and before-after outcomes.
Content freshness: blog activity, service page depth, landing page relevance, and internal linking.

sections.title: How to turn evidence into outreach
sections.copy: A good prospecting workflow turns one observation into a compact chain: source, signal, business implication, first line, and suggested next step. That chain is what makes the outreach feel researched instead of generated.
items: Write the first line from a specific page observation.
State the likely business implication without overclaiming.
Offer a small next step, such as sending three ideas, not a full redesign pitch.

example.title: Example website prospecting note
example.copy: Northstar Analytics explains its reporting product clearly, but the demo CTA and finance proof are not visible until after the first scroll.
items: Signal: Demo CTA appears late on the homepage.
Business angle: High-intent visitors may understand the product without knowing why to act now.
Outreach offer: Send three quick ideas for the hero CTA, proof block, and case-study path.

faqs.question: Is website prospecting the same as website scraping?
faqs.answer: No. Website prospecting uses visible public website information to qualify a prospect and prepare better outreach. It does not require scraping social networks or buying contact databases.

faqs.question: Who should use website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for web design freelancers, SEO consultants, growth consultants, CRO specialists, and solo founders because their offers can be tied directly to website observations.

faqs.question: What should be saved before export?
faqs.answer: Save the company, website URL, fit score, source-backed signals, first line, contact path, and the reason the prospect is worth outreach.

en.title: AI website prospecting tool: qualify prospects before outreach
en.seoTitle: AI Website Prospecting Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
en.description: Use AI website prospecting to find opportunity signals, score prospect fit, and prepare source-backed first lines before outreach.
en.intent: Define the LeadCue method for solo professionals who research prospect websites before outreach.
heroBullets: Start with what the prospect's website already proves instead of a generic contact record.
Use opportunity signals and fit scoring to decide whether the prospect deserves outreach.
Turn the research into a Prospect Card that carries context into CSV or email workflows.

sections.title: What AI website prospecting changes
sections.copy: Traditional outbound often starts with a list and searches for a contact. AI website prospecting starts with the company website and asks whether there is a credible reason to contact the prospect at all.
items: The first decision is qualification, not email discovery.
The research output is a source-backed reason to contact, not a vague personalization line.
The handoff keeps website evidence, fit score, first line, and export fields together.

sections.title: Workflow matrix
sections.copy: The workflow should move from public evidence to prospect judgment to outreach preparation without losing context between tools.
items: Review: inspect homepage, proof, CTA, service pages, content freshness, and contact path.
Qualify: score ICP fit, signal strength, confidence, and outreach readiness.
Prepare: save a Prospect Card with source notes, first line, outreach angle, follow-up status, and export status.

sections.title: When it is the right motion
sections.copy: Website prospecting is strongest when your offer can be connected to visible website improvements or missed opportunities.
items: Web design freelancers can use CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion signals.
SEO consultants can use thin pages, stale content, weak internal paths, and missing intent pages.
Growth consultants can use positioning, campaign path, lead magnet, and proof gaps.

example.title: Website-first research example
example.copy: Instead of saving a SaaS company because it matches an industry filter, you save it because the site has a clear product story, hidden finance proof, and no above-the-fold demo CTA.
items: Signal: proof and CTA are not visible when intent is highest.
Fit: your offer is conversion-focused website improvements.
Output: a Prospect Card with a verifiable first line and export-ready note.

faqs.question: Is AI website prospecting the same as scraping?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow uses visible public website evidence, human review, and source-backed notes. It is not designed to scrape social platforms or build hidden contact intelligence.

faqs.question: Who should use AI website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for SEO consultants, web design freelancers, CRO specialists, growth consultants, positioning consultants, and solo founders because their offers can be tied to website evidence.

faqs.question: What should be produced before outreach starts?
faqs.answer: A saved Prospect Card should include fit score, opportunity signals, source notes, first line, reason to contact, and export status.

en.title: AI prospect research tool: the step before cold email
en.seoTitle: AI Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
en.description: Learn why solo professionals need a prospect research tool that qualifies companies, preserves source evidence, and prepares outreach context before sending cold email.
en.intent: Position LeadCue as the research layer between prospect lists and outreach execution.
heroBullets: A spreadsheet stores prospects after they are accepted; a research layer decides whether they should be accepted.
An email tool sends messages; a research layer prepares the source-backed reason to send one.
A website research layer keeps Prospect Cards, fit scores, and opportunity signals together.

sections.title: Where the research layer sits
sections.copy: Many solo professionals have contact sources, spreadsheets, and email tools, but no dedicated place to decide whether a website-backed prospect is worth outreach.
items: Before export: qualify the website and avoid adding weak prospects.
Before email: prepare first lines and context that can be verified.
Before follow-up: preserve why the prospect was selected, skipped, or reviewed.

sections.title: Decision table for tool boundaries
sections.copy: A focused research layer should not pretend to replace every outbound tool. Its value is to improve the quality of prospects and context handed downstream.
items: Contact database: finds people or emails; research layer explains why the company is worth contacting.
Pipeline tool: manages follow-up status; research layer creates the source-backed context before export.
Email sequencer: sends campaigns; research layer prepares claims, first lines, and notes before sending.

sections.title: Signals the layer should preserve
sections.copy: The layer becomes useful when every saved prospect carries enough context for you to understand the decision later without repeating the research.
items: Website evidence: page observation, source path, and buyer implication.
Qualification: ICP fit, confidence, save threshold, and opportunity category.
Handoff: first line, short email context, export fields, status, and follow-up notes.

example.title: Research layer handoff example
example.copy: You review a website, save a Prospect Card, and export only the qualified prospect with fit score, source notes, first line, and a short outreach angle attached.
items: The exported row receives context, not just a URL.
The email writer can verify the claim before sending.
You can review why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Does a website research layer replace an outreach tool?
faqs.answer: No. It sits before your outreach workflow and prepares qualified, source-backed prospect context for export or handoff.

faqs.question: Does it replace an email finder?
faqs.answer: No. Email finders help locate contacts. A website research layer helps explain whether the prospect is worth contacting and what the outreach should reference.

faqs.question: What makes the layer useful for solo professionals?
faqs.answer: Solo professionals can connect their offer to visible website opportunities, then save that evidence as a reusable Prospect Card.

en.title: Website opportunity signals solo professionals can use before outreach
en.seoTitle: Website Opportunity Signals for Solo Outreach | LeadCue
en.description: Use website opportunity signals to spot CTA, proof, SEO, content, positioning, and timing cues that can support source-backed personal outreach.
en.intent: Give solo professionals a signal taxonomy for turning website observations into outreach reasons.
heroBullets: Opportunity signals connect a visible page observation to a business-relevant outreach angle.
The best signals are specific enough for a buyer to verify and relevant enough for your offer.
Weak signals should trigger review or skip decisions instead of automatic export.

sections.title: Signal taxonomy
sections.copy: A useful taxonomy keeps you from treating subjective design opinions as sales evidence. Each signal should map to a buyer outcome and your offer.
items: Conversion signals: hidden CTA, unclear next step, confusing navigation, weak contact path.
Trust signals: missing case studies, buried proof, no results, weak testimonials, unclear customer segments.
Content signals: stale blog, thin service pages, missing use cases, weak internal linking, outdated resources.

sections.title: How to score signal strength
sections.copy: Signal strength depends on visibility, relevance, and confidence. A visible issue that matches your offer is stronger than a broad guess about the business.
items: Strong: the source is visible, the implication is clear, and the offer can help.
Medium: the pattern is plausible but needs another source or manual review.
Weak: the note is subjective, generic, or disconnected from your offer.

sections.title: Turn signals into outreach context
sections.copy: Signals become useful when they are converted into a reason to contact, a first line, and a Prospect Card note you can verify later.
items: Observation: what the page shows and where it appears.
Implication: why it may affect conversion, trust, discovery, or buyer confidence.
Next step: a low-pressure suggestion such as sending three ideas or a short teardown.

example.title: Opportunity signal example
example.copy: The product page is clear, but the strongest customer proof appears below the main CTA and the navigation does not surface industry-specific examples.
items: Category: trust proof and conversion path.
Reason to contact: buyers may need proof earlier before booking.
First line: mention the proof placement and offer a small set of ideas.

faqs.question: How many opportunity signals should a prospect need?
faqs.answer: One strong signal can be enough for a first touch. Two or three source-backed signals make qualification more reliable.

faqs.question: Are opportunity signals the same as a full audit?
faqs.answer: No. They are lightweight sales research cues used before outreach. A full audit can happen after the prospect engages.

faqs.question: What should happen when the signal is weak?
faqs.answer: Do not export the prospect automatically. Mark it for review, scan another page, or skip until a stronger reason to contact appears.

en.title: How to find a reason to contact a prospect from their website
en.seoTitle: Find a Reason to Contact Prospects from Website Evidence | LeadCue
en.description: Find a credible reason to contact a prospect by reviewing website signals, qualifying fit, and turning source evidence into a useful first line.
en.intent: Help solo operators solve the core problem of why this prospect should be contacted now.
heroBullets: A good reason to contact is specific, visible, relevant to the offer, and easy to verify.
The website should provide evidence, not just a name to personalize around.
If there is no credible reason, the prospect should be skipped or reviewed instead of exported.

sections.title: A simple qualification order
sections.copy: Start with prospect fit, then inspect the website for evidence, then decide whether the observation supports a useful first touch.
items: Fit: does the company match your ICP, offer, and target market?
Evidence: does the website show a specific opportunity signal or gap?
Message: can the observation become a first line and next step without overclaiming?

sections.title: Reason-to-contact checklist
sections.copy: Before saving the prospect, you should be able to answer five questions that make the outreach defensible.
items: What did we see on the website, and where did we see it?
Why might it matter to the buyer?
Why is your offer relevant to that observation?

sections.title: Bad reasons to avoid
sections.copy: Weak reasons make outreach feel automated even when a first line is technically personalized. The problem is not word choice; it is lack of relevance.
items: Vague compliments such as liking the website without a specific observation.
Unverified claims about performance, rankings, revenue, or conversion impact.
Signals that do not connect to your offer or buyer outcome.

example.title: Reason to contact example
example.copy: The website targets finance buyers, but the homepage does not surface finance proof or a clear demo CTA above the first scroll.
items: Observation: proof and CTA placement are visible website details.
Implication: high-intent visitors may not see enough trust before acting.
Next step: offer three hero and proof-section ideas.

faqs.question: What if a website has no obvious issue?
faqs.answer: Do not force a reason. Scan another important page, review a different offer angle, or skip the prospect until stronger evidence appears.

faqs.question: Should the first email include every reason?
faqs.answer: No. Usually one strong reason is enough. Too many observations can make a first touch feel like an unsolicited audit.

faqs.question: Can a reason to contact be positive?
faqs.answer: Yes, if it is specific and relevant. The reason can be an opportunity to build on a strength, not only a problem to fix.

en.title: Source-backed prospect notes for safer personal outreach
en.seoTitle: Source-Backed Prospect Notes for Personal Outreach | LeadCue
en.description: Create source-backed prospect notes that keep website evidence, uncertainty, first lines, and export context attached to each saved prospect.
en.intent: Show solo professionals how to preserve evidence so outreach copy can be reviewed before sending.
heroBullets: Source-backed notes help you verify claims before they appear in outreach.
They reduce generic personalization by tying each first line to a visible page observation.
They make export safer because context travels with the prospect.

sections.title: What source-backed means
sections.copy: A source-backed note states what was observed, where it was observed, why it may matter, and how confident you should be before using it in outreach.
items: Source: page type, URL path, section, or visible website area.
Observation: the exact cue, not a broad interpretation.
Confidence: whether the evidence is strong, incomplete, or needs manual review.

sections.title: Note structure
sections.copy: The best notes are compact enough for export but complete enough that you can understand and verify the outreach angle later.
items: Observed cue: demo CTA appears below the first scroll.
Possible implication: visitors may need a clearer next step when intent is highest.
Outbound use: ask permission to send three hero CTA and proof-section ideas.

sections.title: Review before sending
sections.copy: Source-backed notes should improve judgment, not remove it. Review the source and remove any claim that cannot be supported.
items: Avoid revenue, ranking, or conversion claims without evidence.
Keep uncertainty visible when a signal is incomplete.
Use the note as context, not as a final message that must be sent unchanged.

example.title: Source-backed note example
example.copy: Homepage review: the product story is clear, but the finance proof and demo CTA appear after the first scroll. Possible angle: make buyer trust and next step visible earlier.
items: Specific source: homepage first viewport.
Clear implication: proof and next step visibility.
Safe use: offer ideas without claiming confirmed conversion loss.

faqs.question: Should source notes be exported?
faqs.answer: Yes. Put short source notes in export fields or notes so you can verify why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Can AI-generated notes be used directly?
faqs.answer: They should be reviewed first. The note must match visible evidence before it becomes outreach copy.

faqs.question: How long should a source-backed note be?
faqs.answer: Long enough to preserve the observation, implication, and source, but short enough to scan during outreach review.

en.title: ICP fit score for solo website prospecting
en.seoTitle: ICP Fit Score for Solo Website Prospecting | LeadCue
en.description: Build an ICP fit score that combines prospect fit, website opportunity signals, evidence confidence, and export readiness before personal outreach.
en.intent: Help solo professionals separate prospect fit from signal confidence and outreach readiness.
heroBullets: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer.
Confidence score answers whether the website evidence is strong enough to use.
Export readiness answers whether the prospect can move downstream without rework.

sections.title: Fit score model
sections.copy: A practical score should combine the prospect profile with website evidence and handoff readiness instead of treating all signals as equal.
items: 40 points: ICP match across industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
35 points: opportunity signal quality, source specificity, and buyer implication.
25 points: confidence, contact path, first-line readiness, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: Decision thresholds
sections.copy: Thresholds help you avoid exporting weak prospects while still preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: 80-100: save and prioritize for outreach after source review.
65-79: review manually or scan another page before export.
Below 65: skip, archive, or revisit when the ICP or offer changes.

sections.title: Common scoring mistakes
sections.copy: A high fit score is only useful when the evidence is strong enough to support a real outreach reason.
items: Giving high scores to prospects that match the industry but have no visible reason to contact.
Mixing prospect fit with confidence so weak evidence looks stronger than it is.
Exporting prospects before first line, source notes, and export fields are complete.

example.title: Fit score example
example.copy: A B2B SaaS company matches your ICP, shows a hidden demo CTA and missing proof path, and has enough source evidence for a first line.
items: ICP fit: 36/40.
Signal quality: 28/35.
Readiness: 21/25. Total: 85, save after review.

faqs.question: Is fit score the same as confidence score?
faqs.answer: No. Fit score measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust.

faqs.question: What score should qualify a lead?
faqs.answer: Start with 80 for high-priority outreach, then adjust based on reply quality, booked meetings, and manual QA results.

faqs.question: Should low-score leads be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Keep them out of outreach, but use their notes to improve ICP rules or review later.

en.title: How to choose a prospect research tool for solo professionals
en.seoTitle: Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
en.description: Compare prospect research tools by fit scoring, website evidence, outreach context, review workflows, and export-ready fields for personal outreach.
en.intent: Help buyers evaluate prospect research software for a personal workflow.
en.primaryKeyword: prospect research tool for solo professionals
heroBullets: A research tool should improve lead quality, not just increase lead volume.
The output should explain why a prospect is worth contacting.
Exports should carry source notes into the tools you already use.

sections.title: The job the tool should do
sections.copy: A prospect research tool should help you decide whether a company deserves outreach. That means it needs to combine ICP fit, website evidence, outreach angle, and handoff fields instead of only collecting company names.
items: Qualify prospects before they enter outreach.
Show the source behind each sales cue.
Create copy that references a real page observation.

sections.title: Features that matter
sections.copy: The most useful features sit between research and outreach. They help the operator move from a website scan to a saved prospect card, then to a CSV row without losing context.
items: Fit score and confidence score for prioritization.
Website cues grouped by web design, SEO, marketing, and timing.
Export fields that map to a spreadsheet, email workflow, or custom template.

sections.title: Tool evaluation matrix
sections.copy: When comparing prospect research tools, separate contact discovery from website-backed qualification so you do not buy a list tool for a research-quality problem.
items: Contact discovery: useful when the bottleneck is finding people or emails.
Website research layer: useful when the bottleneck is deciding why the prospect is worth contacting.
Outbound handoff: useful when you need first lines, source notes, fit score, and export-ready fields together.

sections.title: Red flags when comparing tools
sections.copy: Avoid tools that create the illusion of volume while hiding why the prospect matters. If every output looks generic, you will still spend time rewriting notes and guessing at relevance.
items: No visible source notes or page references.
No distinction between fit score and data confidence.
No practical handoff into sheets or outreach tools.

example.title: Evaluation checklist
example.copy: Before adopting a prospect research tool, run five real websites through it and compare whether the output helps you decide who to contact first.
items: Can the tool explain why the prospect fits your ICP?
Can you copy a credible first line without editing from scratch?
Can exported fields preserve the evidence behind the outreach angle?

faqs.question: Should I use a lead database or a research tool?
faqs.answer: Use a database when you only need contact discovery. Use a research tool when the bottleneck is knowing which prospects deserve outreach and what to say.

faqs.question: How many fields should a prospect card include?
faqs.answer: Enough to preserve context: company, URL, fit score, confidence, signals, first line, contact path, source notes, status, and export status.

faqs.question: Does AI-generated research need human review?
faqs.answer: Yes. AI should reduce research time, but you should review source notes before sending outreach or saving a prospect as qualified.

en.title: Cold email first lines from real website evidence
en.seoTitle: Cold Email First Lines from Website Evidence | LeadCue
en.description: Write personalized cold email first lines from prospect websites, visible page signals, and source-backed business angles.
en.intent: Teach solo operators how to create first lines that feel researched.
en.primaryKeyword: cold email first lines from website
heroBullets: Good first lines are specific, visible, and relevant to the offer.
The line should create context, not pretend familiarity.
The next sentence should connect the observation to a useful business outcome.

sections.title: What makes a first line work
sections.copy: A strong first line proves that the sender looked at the company and noticed something relevant. It should be short enough to scan, specific enough to be credible, and connected to the reason for the email.
items: Reference a visible website detail rather than a vague compliment.
Avoid fake praise such as 'loved your website' without evidence.
Use the first line to set up the problem or opportunity your offer addresses.

sections.title: Use website evidence, not personalization theater
sections.copy: Many first lines fail because they mention a podcast, LinkedIn post, or award that has nothing to do with the offer. Website evidence works better for solo outreach because it points directly to marketing, design, SEO, or conversion opportunities.
items: CTA placement can support a conversion angle.
Inactive content can support an SEO or demand generation angle.
Missing proof can support a messaging or redesign angle.

sections.title: A simple formula
sections.copy: Use this pattern: 'I noticed [specific website observation], which may affect [buyer outcome].' Then offer a small next step that reduces friction for the recipient.
items: Observation: what you saw and where you saw it.
Implication: why it may matter to the prospect.
Offer: a low-pressure next step, such as sending ideas or a short teardown.

sections.title: Before and after first-line library
sections.copy: First lines should improve when the source note becomes more specific. Use examples to separate credible website evidence from generic personalization.
items: Weak: I loved your website. Stronger: I noticed the demo CTA appears after the first scroll.
Weak: Your blog looks interesting. Stronger: I saw the resources section has not published a buyer guide recently.
Weak: You have a great product. Stronger: The product story is clear, but finance proof is not visible near the main CTA.

example.title: Before and after
example.copy: Weak: 'I loved your website.' Stronger: 'I noticed the product story is clear, but the demo CTA and finance proof show up after the first scroll.'
items: The stronger line is specific.
It names the business context.
It gives the next sentence a natural reason to exist.

faqs.question: Should every cold email include a first line?
faqs.answer: If the prospect is worth personalized outreach, yes. If you cannot find a relevant observation, the prospect may not be ready for a high-effort touch.

faqs.question: Can AI write first lines?
faqs.answer: AI can draft first lines quickly, but the best outputs still need source notes so the rep can verify that the observation is real.

faqs.question: How long should a first line be?
faqs.answer: Long enough to be specific, short enough to read on mobile. There is no SEO or email performance magic in a fixed word count.

en.title: Prospect qualification before outreach starts
en.description: Score prospects with website signals, fit evidence, confidence, and a clear reason to contact before saving another lead.
en.intent: Help solo professionals define what makes a prospect worth saving and exporting.
heroBullets: Qualification should happen before a prospect enters personal outreach.
A saved prospect needs both ICP fit and a reason to reach out.
Confidence matters because thin page data can make a signal weaker.

sections.title: Separate fit from evidence quality
sections.copy: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer. Confidence answers whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust the recommendation. Both are needed before you save the prospect.
items: Fit includes industry, company type, service need, and offer relevance.
Confidence includes page depth, source clarity, and signal specificity.
A high-fit prospect with weak evidence may need manual review before outreach.

sections.title: Define your save threshold
sections.copy: Set a clear threshold for when a prospect is worth saving. Without one, it is easy to keep too many companies that have no usable outreach angle.
items: Save when fit score is strong and at least two website cues support the angle.
Review when fit is strong but confidence is medium.
Skip when the only available notes are generic or unrelated to your offer.

sections.title: Qualification matrix
sections.copy: A clear matrix keeps high-fit prospects with weak evidence out of outreach while preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: High fit and high confidence: save as a Prospect Card and prioritize outreach.
High fit and low confidence: review another page before export.
Low fit and strong signal: archive the note unless your offer or ICP changes.

sections.title: Carry qualification into export
sections.copy: The qualification context should not disappear when a prospect leaves the research tool. Export fields should include fit score, signal summary, first line, source notes, follow-up status, and saved/exported status.
items: Use status fields to prevent duplicate work.
Keep source notes in exports or your own notes for review.
Export only saved prospects when building outreach lists.

example.title: A practical save rule
example.copy: Save a prospect when it matches the ICP, has at least two source-backed website cues, and has a first line you would be comfortable sending.
items: Fit score: 80 or higher.
Confidence: 70% or higher.
Status: saved, reviewed, and ready for CSV export.

faqs.question: What is a good fit score threshold?
faqs.answer: Start with 80 for high-priority outreach, then adjust based on reply quality and booked meetings rather than scan volume alone.

faqs.question: Should weak-fit prospects be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not always. Keep them out of immediate outreach, but use notes to understand whether the ICP or scoring rules need refinement.

faqs.question: How should solo professionals review qualification quality?
faqs.answer: Review saved prospects weekly by fit score, confidence, reply quality, and whether the first line matched a real source note.

en.title: Website prospecting for web design freelancers
en.seoTitle: Website Prospecting for Web Design Freelancers | LeadCue
en.description: Find web design prospects by reviewing website clarity, proof, CTA placement, contact paths, and redesign-ready signals.
en.intent: Show web design freelancers how LeadCue supports redesign and conversion outreach.
secondaryKeywords: find businesses that need website redesign
heroBullets: Find redesign reasons that are visible on the prospect site.
Prioritize prospects with conversion and proof gaps.
Write outreach around a specific improvement, not a generic redesign pitch.

sections.title: Best signals for web design offers
sections.copy: A web design freelancer needs signals that point to conversion, clarity, credibility, or user experience. The strongest signals are easy for the prospect to verify because they are visible on the page.
items: Primary CTA is hidden, vague, or inconsistent across the site.
Proof is buried below product explanations or missing from key pages.
Navigation makes it hard for buyers to find services, pricing, demos, or case studies.

sections.title: How to package the outreach
sections.copy: Do not lead with 'your website needs a redesign.' Lead with a helpful observation and a small set of ideas. That gives the buyer value before asking for a meeting.
items: Use a first line based on the page observation.
Offer three conversion ideas or a short homepage teardown.
Mention the specific page section you reviewed.

sections.title: What to save for yourself
sections.copy: Every saved prospect should help you understand the redesign angle quickly when you come back later. A Prospect Card should include the signal, the source, and the likely business impact.
items: Fit score and confidence.
Top web design cues.
Recommended first line and source-backed note.

example.title: Redesign outreach angle
example.copy: The homepage explains the product, but the strongest proof and demo CTA show up after the first scroll.
items: Angle: Improve above-the-fold conversion.
Offer: Send a quick hero and proof-section teardown.
CTA: Ask whether they want the three ideas.

faqs.question: What makes a company redesign-ready?
faqs.answer: Look for a strong business that has visible friction on its website: unclear CTA, weak proof, outdated layout, confusing navigation, or low-conviction messaging.

faqs.question: Should redesign outreach mention competitors?
faqs.answer: Only if it is useful and accurate. A direct observation from the prospect's own website is usually safer and more relevant.

faqs.question: Can LeadCue replace a full website audit?
faqs.answer: No. It helps identify and package initial sales angles. A full audit can come later after the prospect engages.

en.title: Prospect research for SEO consultants using website gaps
en.seoTitle: Prospect Research for SEO Consultants | LeadCue
en.description: Help SEO consultants find prospects by spotting content freshness, service page depth, internal linking, and search-demand gaps on real websites.
en.intent: Help SEO consultants use website evidence to prioritize and personalize outbound.
heroBullets: Use visible SEO and content gaps to qualify prospects.
Avoid generic 'we can improve your rankings' outreach.
Export source notes so your outreach keeps the exact angle.

sections.title: SEO signals that support outreach
sections.copy: SEO consultants should focus on signals that a business leader can understand without a technical audit. Public pages often show enough evidence to open a useful conversation.
items: Thin or outdated service pages.
Inactive blog or resource hub.
Missing location, industry, comparison, or use-case pages.

sections.title: How to make the angle credible
sections.copy: A credible SEO outreach angle starts with what you saw and why it could matter. Avoid promising rankings in the opener. Offer a concrete next step such as a short content gap review.
items: Name the page or section you reviewed.
Connect the gap to buyer discovery, trust, or conversion.
Offer to send a few page ideas or keyword themes.

sections.title: What to export
sections.copy: SEO prospect notes should be reusable when you write outreach or follow up. Keep the page source, category, and recommended first line together.
items: Primary website gap.
Recommended content angle.
Source URL and qualification confidence.

example.title: SEO outreach angle
example.copy: The blog appears inactive and the main navigation does not surface use-case or comparison content for finance buyers.
items: Angle: Content depth for buyer research.
Offer: Send three page ideas tied to high-intent searches.
CTA: Ask if they want the short list.

faqs.question: Should SEO prospecting require a full crawl?
faqs.answer: Not for the first touch. A light website scan can find enough evidence to decide whether the prospect is worth deeper review.

faqs.question: What SEO signals are too technical for cold outreach?
faqs.answer: Avoid leading with obscure technical details unless the buyer is technical. Start with visible business impact such as thin service pages or stale content.

faqs.question: How can SEO consultants avoid generic first lines?
faqs.answer: Tie the first line to a specific page gap, content absence, or buyer journey issue rather than a broad claim about rankings.

en.title: Website prospecting for marketing consultants
en.seoTitle: Website Prospecting for Marketing Consultants | LeadCue
en.description: Qualify marketing prospects from conversion signals, trust proof, campaign paths, and website evidence before outreach.
en.intent: Show marketing consultants and solo founders how to build evidence-backed outreach from company websites.
heroBullets: Find campaign angles before a company enters outreach.
Use positioning and proof gaps to write useful first touches.
Save notes that explain why the prospect is a fit.

sections.title: Signals for marketing offers
sections.copy: Marketing consultants need signals that reveal demand generation, conversion, or messaging opportunities. The public website often shows whether a company is ready for better campaigns.
items: Unclear positioning or vague value proposition.
No lead magnet, demo path, webinar, guide, or conversion offer.
Proof and customer outcomes are missing from key buyer pages.

sections.title: Turn gaps into a useful offer
sections.copy: The best marketing outreach does not simply say 'we can get you leads.' It points to a visible friction point and offers a small asset or campaign idea the prospect can evaluate.
items: Offer a landing page angle for a specific segment.
Suggest a proof section or lead magnet tied to buyer intent.
Send a short teardown instead of asking for a discovery call immediately.

sections.title: Build a repeatable research motion
sections.copy: Solo operators can review more prospects when each saved Prospect Card uses the same fields. Consistency makes it easier to compare opportunities and improve outreach quality.
items: Score fit against your ICP.
Classify signals as positioning, proof, campaign, or conversion.
Export only prospects with a concrete opening angle.

example.title: Growth outreach angle
example.copy: The website explains the product, but there is no obvious buyer guide, lead magnet, or case-study path for visitors who are not ready to book a demo.
items: Angle: Capture mid-funnel demand.
Offer: Send two lead magnet concepts and a page path.
CTA: Ask whether they want the quick ideas.

faqs.question: What makes website prospecting useful for growth consultants?
faqs.answer: It turns visible page gaps into campaign ideas, which makes outreach more relevant than a generic promise of more leads.

faqs.question: Should marketing consultants focus on fit or urgency?
faqs.answer: Both. Fit tells you the company matches your offer. Website signals help explain why the timing may be right.

faqs.question: What should be included in a marketing prospect card?
faqs.answer: Include fit score, campaign angle, proof gap, first line, source notes, and the recommended next step.

en.title: How to turn a prospect website into a cold email angle
en.seoTitle: Turn a Prospect Website into a Cold Email Angle | LeadCue
en.description: Turn prospect website observations into a specific cold email angle, first line, and low-pressure next step.
en.intent: Give operators a repeatable workflow for building outreach angles from websites.
heroBullets: Scan the website for one concrete business-relevant observation.
Connect the observation to a likely buyer outcome.
Offer a small next step that makes replying easy.

sections.title: Step 1: Choose the right page
sections.copy: Start with the homepage, pricing page, product page, services page, or main navigation. These pages usually reveal the strongest business signals without needing a full audit.
items: Review headline, CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion path.
Look for buyer friction, not minor design preferences.
Capture the source so the note can be checked later.

sections.title: Step 2: Translate the observation
sections.copy: A website observation becomes an outreach angle when it explains a possible business effect. The goal is not to diagnose everything; it is to create a credible reason to start a conversation.
items: Observation: the demo CTA is below the first scroll.
Possible effect: high-intent visitors may not act while attention is highest.
Offer: send three hero section ideas.

sections.title: Step 3: Write the email
sections.copy: Keep the email compact. Use the first line to prove relevance, the second sentence to explain the opportunity, and the CTA to ask permission to send ideas.
items: Do not overstate certainty from a light scan.
Do not pitch every service in the first touch.
Make the reply easy: 'Want me to send them over?'

example.title: Cold email angle example
example.copy: I noticed the product story is clear, but finance buyer proof and the demo CTA show up after the first scroll. That can cost booked demos from visitors who already understand the product.
items: First line: specific and verifiable.
Opportunity: tied to booked demos.
CTA: send three quick fixes.

faqs.question: How many website signals should one email mention?
faqs.answer: Usually one strong signal is enough. Mentioning too many signals can make the email feel like an audit dump.

faqs.question: Should the CTA ask for a meeting?
faqs.answer: For cold first touches, a lighter CTA often works better. Offer to send ideas, a teardown, or examples before asking for a meeting.

faqs.question: What if the website has no obvious issue?
faqs.answer: Do not force it. Either scan another page, save the prospect for later review, or skip it until you have a stronger reason to reach out.

en.title: How to score a prospect website before outreach
en.seoTitle: How to Score a Prospect Website for Outreach | LeadCue
en.description: Use a lightweight website scoring workflow to decide whether a prospect deserves outreach, review, or export.
en.intent: Give solo operators a scoring method they can use before saving and exporting prospects.
heroBullets: Fit score tells you whether the prospect matches your offer.
Signal quality tells you whether the outreach reason is strong.
Export readiness tells you whether the prospect is ready for the next step.

sections.title: Score ICP fit first
sections.copy: Start with the company itself. If it does not match your offer focus, even a clear website gap may not be worth pursuing.
items: Industry and market fit.
Company size or complexity fit.
Offer relevance for web design, SEO, marketing, or growth services.

sections.title: Score the website cues
sections.copy: Website cues should be specific enough to support a first line and meaningful enough to imply a business opportunity. Weak cues are generic, subjective, or unrelated to your offer.
items: Strong cue: demo CTA is not visible above the fold.
Medium cue: blog appears inactive but the source date is unclear.
Weak cue: website could look more modern.

sections.title: Weighted scoring matrix
sections.copy: A weighted model gives you the same decision language each time and makes it easier to tune the workflow after outreach runs.
items: ICP fit: 40 points for industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
Opportunity signal: 35 points for source specificity, business implication, and offer relevance.
Readiness: 25 points for confidence, contact path, first line, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: Score export readiness
sections.copy: A prospect is not ready just because it has a high score. The handoff fields need to be complete enough for you to use without redoing the research.
items: First line is copy-ready.
Source notes are attached.
Follow-up status and export fields are set.

example.title: Simple scoring model
example.copy: Use 40 points for ICP fit, 35 for website cues, 15 for confidence, and 10 for contact path and export readiness.
items: 80-100: save and prioritize.
65-79: review manually before outreach.
Below 65: skip or revisit later.

faqs.question: Should scoring be fully automated?
faqs.answer: Automation should create a recommendation, but you should review edge cases and use reply quality to tune the model.

faqs.question: What is the difference between fit and confidence?
faqs.answer: Fit measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures how strong and complete the available evidence is.

faqs.question: Should low-score leads be exported?
faqs.answer: Usually no. Exporting weak prospects creates downstream work and lowers outreach quality.

en.title: Website audit outreach that sends useful ideas first
en.seoTitle: Website Audit Outreach Guide for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
en.description: Use website audit outreach to send source-backed ideas, start a useful conversation, and avoid generic redesign pitches.
en.intent: Teach solo professionals how to package audit-style observations into better first touches.
heroBullets: A lightweight audit should open a conversation, not overwhelm the buyer.
The best first touch gives one useful observation and one clear next step.
Evidence-backed audit notes can become repeatable sales assets.

sections.title: Keep the audit lightweight
sections.copy: A first-touch audit should not include every issue you can find. It should identify one or two high-leverage improvements the buyer can understand quickly.
items: Focus on the homepage, navigation, proof, CTA, or key service page.
Avoid long PDFs before the prospect asks for detail.
Use language that connects the issue to pipeline, trust, or conversion.

sections.title: Package the idea clearly
sections.copy: The outreach should make the prospect curious enough to reply. A useful structure is: observation, why it may matter, and permission to send ideas.
items: Observation: the case-study path is hard to find.
Implication: buyers may not see proof before deciding whether to book.
CTA: ask if they want three quick improvement ideas.

sections.title: Use audit notes for follow-up
sections.copy: If the prospect does not reply, the source notes still help with follow-up. You can reference the same signal from a new angle without inventing a new reason.
items: Follow up with one example, not a generic bump.
Keep the original source note visible in your notes.
Stop when the prospect no longer has a strong reason to reach out.

example.title: Website audit outreach example
example.copy: I noticed the homepage explains the product clearly, but the proof section sits below the main CTA. I found three ways to make the demo path feel safer for finance buyers.
items: The note is specific.
The buyer outcome is clear.
The CTA is low pressure.

faqs.question: Should a website audit email include screenshots?
faqs.answer: Screenshots can help in later follow-up, but the first email should stay lightweight unless the screenshot is essential to understanding the point.

faqs.question: How long should a first website audit email be?
faqs.answer: Short enough to scan on mobile. The goal is to earn permission to send more detail, not to deliver the full audit immediately.

faqs.question: What if the website already looks good?
faqs.answer: Look for messaging, proof, conversion, or content gaps. If there is no meaningful gap, skip the prospect or use a different offer.

zh.title: 网站客户开发工具：从客户官网判断是否值得开发
zh.seoTitle: 网站客户开发工具与筛选流程 | LeadCue
zh.description: 用客户官网里的 CTA、案例、内容、联系方式和定位信号筛选销售线索，找到开发信切入角度。
zh.intent: 说明如何用客户官网筛选更值得开发的销售线索。
secondaryKeywords: 客户官网开发信个性化
heroBullets: 从客户官网出发，而不是从泛泛的公司名单或邮箱库出发。
用首屏、案例、CTA、内容更新和联系方式判断这个线索是否值得开发。
把每个观察点转成开发信开场白、业务切入角度和 CSV 可用备注。

sections.title: 网站客户开发到底在做什么
sections.copy: 网站客户开发不是简单抓取网页，也不是把域名批量塞进开发信工具。它是在联系客户之前，先阅读客户官网里的公开信号，判断这个公司是否适合你的服务，以及是否有一个自然、具体、可验证的联系理由。
items: 适合网站建设、SEO、CRO、增长营销和定位优化类服务商。
重点不是找更多公司，而是筛出更值得跟进的公司。
好的输出应该解释为什么现在值得联系，而不只是给出一个公司名称。

sections.title: 优先观察哪些官网信号
sections.copy: 高价值信号通常和业务结果有关。比如首屏没有清楚 CTA，可能影响预约转化；案例和客户证明隐藏太深，可能影响信任建立；服务页内容很浅，可能说明 SEO 和转化都有提升空间。
items: 首屏表达：标题、价值主张、CTA 和信任证明是否清楚。
信任资产：案例、客户评价、Logo、结果数据是否容易被买家看到。
内容和导线：博客更新、服务页深度、导航路径和联系入口是否支持转化。

sections.title: 如何转成开发信上下文
sections.copy: 开发信不应该只写“我看了你们的网站”。更有效的做法是把官网观察点串成一个小链条：看到什么、可能影响什么、你能提供什么小建议。这样邮件看起来像真实研究，而不是批量模板。
items: 第一句话引用一个具体页面观察点。
第二句把观察点连接到转化、信任、SEO 或销售结果。
下一步只提出一个轻量请求，例如发送 3 个改进建议。

example.title: 网站客户开发备注示例
example.copy: Northstar Analytics 的产品说明很清楚，但首页首屏没有直接展示预约演示 CTA，面向财务个人用户的客户证明也需要下滑后才看到。
items: 信号：预约演示入口在首屏下方，强意图访客可能需要多走一步。
业务角度：如果买家已经理解产品，却没有立即行动入口，转化可能被延后。
开发信切入：可以发 3 个首屏 CTA、证明模块和案例入口的快速建议。

faqs.question: 网站客户开发和网站抓取是一回事吗？
faqs.answer: 不是。网站客户开发关注公开可见的信息，并用这些信息判断线索质量和开发理由；它不等于批量抓取联系人，也不依赖购买邮箱数据库。

faqs.question: 哪些个人用户最适合这种方法？
faqs.answer: 网站建设、SEO、增长营销、CRO 和品牌定位服务商最适合，因为他们的服务可以直接和客户官网里的问题或机会对应起来。

faqs.question: 应该把哪些内容保存到 CSV？
faqs.answer: 至少保存公司名称、官网 URL、匹配评分、主要网站信号、来源备注、开发信开场白、跟进状态、阶段和导出状态。

zh.title: AI website prospecting tool: qualify prospects before outreach
zh.seoTitle: AI Website Prospecting Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
zh.description: Use AI website prospecting to find opportunity signals, score prospect fit, and prepare source-backed first lines before outreach.
zh.intent: Define the LeadCue method for solo professionals who research prospect websites before outreach.
heroBullets: Start with what the prospect's website already proves instead of a generic contact record.
Use opportunity signals and fit scoring to decide whether the prospect deserves outreach.
Turn the research into a Prospect Card that carries context into CSV or email workflows.

sections.title: What AI website prospecting changes
sections.copy: Traditional outbound often starts with a list and searches for a contact. AI website prospecting starts with the company website and asks whether there is a credible reason to contact the prospect at all.
items: The first decision is qualification, not email discovery.
The research output is a source-backed reason to contact, not a vague personalization line.
The handoff keeps website evidence, fit score, first line, and export fields together.

sections.title: Workflow matrix
sections.copy: The workflow should move from public evidence to prospect judgment to outreach preparation without losing context between tools.
items: Review: inspect homepage, proof, CTA, service pages, content freshness, and contact path.
Qualify: score ICP fit, signal strength, confidence, and outreach readiness.
Prepare: save a Prospect Card with source notes, first line, outreach angle, follow-up status, and export status.

sections.title: When it is the right motion
sections.copy: Website prospecting is strongest when your offer can be connected to visible website improvements or missed opportunities.
items: Web design freelancers can use CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion signals.
SEO consultants can use thin pages, stale content, weak internal paths, and missing intent pages.
Growth consultants can use positioning, campaign path, lead magnet, and proof gaps.

example.title: Website-first research example
example.copy: Instead of saving a SaaS company because it matches an industry filter, you save it because the site has a clear product story, hidden finance proof, and no above-the-fold demo CTA.
items: Signal: proof and CTA are not visible when intent is highest.
Fit: your offer is conversion-focused website improvements.
Output: a Prospect Card with a verifiable first line and export-ready note.

faqs.question: Is AI website prospecting the same as scraping?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow uses visible public website evidence, human review, and source-backed notes. It is not designed to scrape social platforms or build hidden contact intelligence.

faqs.question: Who should use AI website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for SEO consultants, web design freelancers, CRO specialists, growth consultants, positioning consultants, and solo founders because their offers can be tied to website evidence.

faqs.question: What should be produced before outreach starts?
faqs.answer: A saved Prospect Card should include fit score, opportunity signals, source notes, first line, reason to contact, and export status.

zh.title: AI prospect research tool: the step before cold email
zh.seoTitle: AI Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
zh.description: Learn why solo professionals need a prospect research tool that qualifies companies, preserves source evidence, and prepares outreach context before sending cold email.
zh.intent: Position LeadCue as the research layer between prospect lists and outreach execution.
heroBullets: A spreadsheet stores prospects after they are accepted; a research layer decides whether they should be accepted.
An email tool sends messages; a research layer prepares the source-backed reason to send one.
A website research layer keeps Prospect Cards, fit scores, and opportunity signals together.

sections.title: Where the research layer sits
sections.copy: Many solo professionals have contact sources, spreadsheets, and email tools, but no dedicated place to decide whether a website-backed prospect is worth outreach.
items: Before export: qualify the website and avoid adding weak prospects.
Before email: prepare first lines and context that can be verified.
Before follow-up: preserve why the prospect was selected, skipped, or reviewed.

sections.title: Decision table for tool boundaries
sections.copy: A focused research layer should not pretend to replace every outbound tool. Its value is to improve the quality of prospects and context handed downstream.
items: Contact database: finds people or emails; research layer explains why the company is worth contacting.
Pipeline tool: manages follow-up status; research layer creates the source-backed context before export.
Email sequencer: sends campaigns; research layer prepares claims, first lines, and notes before sending.

sections.title: Signals the layer should preserve
sections.copy: The layer becomes useful when every saved prospect carries enough context for you to understand the decision later without repeating the research.
items: Website evidence: page observation, source path, and buyer implication.
Qualification: ICP fit, confidence, save threshold, and opportunity category.
Handoff: first line, short email context, export fields, status, and follow-up notes.

example.title: Research layer handoff example
example.copy: You review a website, save a Prospect Card, and export only the qualified prospect with fit score, source notes, first line, and a short outreach angle attached.
items: The exported row receives context, not just a URL.
The email writer can verify the claim before sending.
You can review why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Does a website research layer replace an outreach tool?
faqs.answer: No. It sits before your outreach workflow and prepares qualified, source-backed prospect context for export or handoff.

faqs.question: Does it replace an email finder?
faqs.answer: No. Email finders help locate contacts. A website research layer helps explain whether the prospect is worth contacting and what the outreach should reference.

faqs.question: What makes the layer useful for solo professionals?
faqs.answer: Solo professionals can connect their offer to visible website opportunities, then save that evidence as a reusable Prospect Card.

zh.title: Website opportunity signals solo professionals can use before outreach
zh.seoTitle: Website Opportunity Signals for Solo Outreach | LeadCue
zh.description: Use website opportunity signals to spot CTA, proof, SEO, content, positioning, and timing cues that can support source-backed personal outreach.
zh.intent: Give solo professionals a signal taxonomy for turning website observations into outreach reasons.
heroBullets: Opportunity signals connect a visible page observation to a business-relevant outreach angle.
The best signals are specific enough for a buyer to verify and relevant enough for your offer.
Weak signals should trigger review or skip decisions instead of automatic export.

sections.title: Signal taxonomy
sections.copy: A useful taxonomy keeps you from treating subjective design opinions as sales evidence. Each signal should map to a buyer outcome and your offer.
items: Conversion signals: hidden CTA, unclear next step, confusing navigation, weak contact path.
Trust signals: missing case studies, buried proof, no results, weak testimonials, unclear customer segments.
Content signals: stale blog, thin service pages, missing use cases, weak internal linking, outdated resources.

sections.title: How to score signal strength
sections.copy: Signal strength depends on visibility, relevance, and confidence. A visible issue that matches your offer is stronger than a broad guess about the business.
items: Strong: the source is visible, the implication is clear, and the offer can help.
Medium: the pattern is plausible but needs another source or manual review.
Weak: the note is subjective, generic, or disconnected from your offer.

sections.title: Turn signals into outreach context
sections.copy: Signals become useful when they are converted into a reason to contact, a first line, and a Prospect Card note you can verify later.
items: Observation: what the page shows and where it appears.
Implication: why it may affect conversion, trust, discovery, or buyer confidence.
Next step: a low-pressure suggestion such as sending three ideas or a short teardown.

example.title: Opportunity signal example
example.copy: The product page is clear, but the strongest customer proof appears below the main CTA and the navigation does not surface industry-specific examples.
items: Category: trust proof and conversion path.
Reason to contact: buyers may need proof earlier before booking.
First line: mention the proof placement and offer a small set of ideas.

faqs.question: How many opportunity signals should a prospect need?
faqs.answer: One strong signal can be enough for a first touch. Two or three source-backed signals make qualification more reliable.

faqs.question: Are opportunity signals the same as a full audit?
faqs.answer: No. They are lightweight sales research cues used before outreach. A full audit can happen after the prospect engages.

faqs.question: What should happen when the signal is weak?
faqs.answer: Do not export the prospect automatically. Mark it for review, scan another page, or skip until a stronger reason to contact appears.

zh.title: How to find a reason to contact a prospect from their website
zh.seoTitle: Find a Reason to Contact Prospects from Website Evidence | LeadCue
zh.description: Find a credible reason to contact a prospect by reviewing website signals, qualifying fit, and turning source evidence into a useful first line.
zh.intent: Help solo operators solve the core problem of why this prospect should be contacted now.
heroBullets: A good reason to contact is specific, visible, relevant to the offer, and easy to verify.
The website should provide evidence, not just a name to personalize around.
If there is no credible reason, the prospect should be skipped or reviewed instead of exported.

sections.title: A simple qualification order
sections.copy: Start with prospect fit, then inspect the website for evidence, then decide whether the observation supports a useful first touch.
items: Fit: does the company match your ICP, offer, and target market?
Evidence: does the website show a specific opportunity signal or gap?
Message: can the observation become a first line and next step without overclaiming?

sections.title: Reason-to-contact checklist
sections.copy: Before saving the prospect, you should be able to answer five questions that make the outreach defensible.
items: What did we see on the website, and where did we see it?
Why might it matter to the buyer?
Why is your offer relevant to that observation?

sections.title: Bad reasons to avoid
sections.copy: Weak reasons make outreach feel automated even when a first line is technically personalized. The problem is not word choice; it is lack of relevance.
items: Vague compliments such as liking the website without a specific observation.
Unverified claims about performance, rankings, revenue, or conversion impact.
Signals that do not connect to your offer or buyer outcome.

example.title: Reason to contact example
example.copy: The website targets finance buyers, but the homepage does not surface finance proof or a clear demo CTA above the first scroll.
items: Observation: proof and CTA placement are visible website details.
Implication: high-intent visitors may not see enough trust before acting.
Next step: offer three hero and proof-section ideas.

faqs.question: What if a website has no obvious issue?
faqs.answer: Do not force a reason. Scan another important page, review a different offer angle, or skip the prospect until stronger evidence appears.

faqs.question: Should the first email include every reason?
faqs.answer: No. Usually one strong reason is enough. Too many observations can make a first touch feel like an unsolicited audit.

faqs.question: Can a reason to contact be positive?
faqs.answer: Yes, if it is specific and relevant. The reason can be an opportunity to build on a strength, not only a problem to fix.

zh.title: Source-backed prospect notes for safer personal outreach
zh.seoTitle: Source-Backed Prospect Notes for Personal Outreach | LeadCue
zh.description: Create source-backed prospect notes that keep website evidence, uncertainty, first lines, and export context attached to each saved prospect.
zh.intent: Show solo professionals how to preserve evidence so outreach copy can be reviewed before sending.
heroBullets: Source-backed notes help you verify claims before they appear in outreach.
They reduce generic personalization by tying each first line to a visible page observation.
They make export safer because context travels with the prospect.

sections.title: What source-backed means
sections.copy: A source-backed note states what was observed, where it was observed, why it may matter, and how confident you should be before using it in outreach.
items: Source: page type, URL path, section, or visible website area.
Observation: the exact cue, not a broad interpretation.
Confidence: whether the evidence is strong, incomplete, or needs manual review.

sections.title: Note structure
sections.copy: The best notes are compact enough for export but complete enough that you can understand and verify the outreach angle later.
items: Observed cue: demo CTA appears below the first scroll.
Possible implication: visitors may need a clearer next step when intent is highest.
Outbound use: ask permission to send three hero CTA and proof-section ideas.

sections.title: Review before sending
sections.copy: Source-backed notes should improve judgment, not remove it. Review the source and remove any claim that cannot be supported.
items: Avoid revenue, ranking, or conversion claims without evidence.
Keep uncertainty visible when a signal is incomplete.
Use the note as context, not as a final message that must be sent unchanged.

example.title: Source-backed note example
example.copy: Homepage review: the product story is clear, but the finance proof and demo CTA appear after the first scroll. Possible angle: make buyer trust and next step visible earlier.
items: Specific source: homepage first viewport.
Clear implication: proof and next step visibility.
Safe use: offer ideas without claiming confirmed conversion loss.

faqs.question: Should source notes be exported?
faqs.answer: Yes. Put short source notes in export fields or notes so you can verify why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Can AI-generated notes be used directly?
faqs.answer: They should be reviewed first. The note must match visible evidence before it becomes outreach copy.

faqs.question: How long should a source-backed note be?
faqs.answer: Long enough to preserve the observation, implication, and source, but short enough to scan during outreach review.

zh.title: ICP fit score for solo website prospecting
zh.seoTitle: ICP Fit Score for Solo Website Prospecting | LeadCue
zh.description: Build an ICP fit score that combines prospect fit, website opportunity signals, evidence confidence, and export readiness before personal outreach.
zh.intent: Help solo professionals separate prospect fit from signal confidence and outreach readiness.
heroBullets: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer.
Confidence score answers whether the website evidence is strong enough to use.
Export readiness answers whether the prospect can move downstream without rework.

sections.title: Fit score model
sections.copy: A practical score should combine the prospect profile with website evidence and handoff readiness instead of treating all signals as equal.
items: 40 points: ICP match across industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
35 points: opportunity signal quality, source specificity, and buyer implication.
25 points: confidence, contact path, first-line readiness, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: Decision thresholds
sections.copy: Thresholds help you avoid exporting weak prospects while still preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: 80-100: save and prioritize for outreach after source review.
65-79: review manually or scan another page before export.
Below 65: skip, archive, or revisit when the ICP or offer changes.

sections.title: Common scoring mistakes
sections.copy: A high fit score is only useful when the evidence is strong enough to support a real outreach reason.
items: Giving high scores to prospects that match the industry but have no visible reason to contact.
Mixing prospect fit with confidence so weak evidence looks stronger than it is.
Exporting prospects before first line, source notes, and export fields are complete.

example.title: Fit score example
example.copy: A B2B SaaS company matches your ICP, shows a hidden demo CTA and missing proof path, and has enough source evidence for a first line.
items: ICP fit: 36/40.
Signal quality: 28/35.
Readiness: 21/25. Total: 85, save after review.

faqs.question: Is fit score the same as confidence score?
faqs.answer: No. Fit score measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust.

faqs.question: What score should qualify a lead?
faqs.answer: Start with 80 for high-priority outreach, then adjust based on reply quality, booked meetings, and manual QA results.

faqs.question: Should low-score leads be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Keep them out of outreach, but use their notes to improve ICP rules or review later.

zh.title: 个人专业用户如何选择销售线索筛选工具
zh.seoTitle: 销售线索筛选工具选型指南 | LeadCue
zh.description: 面向个人专业用户的线索筛选工具应保留网站证据、匹配评分、开发信角度和 CSV 导出字段，而不只是收集公司名单。
zh.intent: Help buyers evaluate prospect research software for a personal workflow.
heroBullets: 研究工具应该提高潜在客户质量，而不仅仅是增加潜在客户数量。
输出应解释为什么值得联系潜在客户。
Exports should carry source notes into the tools you already use.

sections.title: 工具应该完成的工作
sections.copy: A prospect research tool should help you decide whether a company deserves outreach. That means it needs to combine ICP fit, website evidence, outreach angle, and handoff fields instead of only collecting company names.
items: Qualify prospects before they enter outreach.
显示每个销售线索背后的来源。
创建引用真实页面观察的副本。

sections.title: 重要的功能
sections.copy: The most useful features sit between research and outreach. They help the operator move from a website scan to a saved prospect card, then to a CSV row without losing context.
items: 优先级的拟合得分和置信度得分。
网站线索按网页设计、SEO、营销和时机分组。
Export fields that map to a spreadsheet, email workflow, or custom template.

sections.title: Tool evaluation matrix
sections.copy: When comparing prospect research tools, separate contact discovery from website-backed qualification so you do not buy a list tool for a research-quality problem.
items: Contact discovery: useful when the bottleneck is finding people or emails.
Website research layer: useful when the bottleneck is deciding why the prospect is worth contacting.
Outbound handoff: useful when you need first lines, source notes, fit score, and export-ready fields together.

sections.title: 比较工具时的危险信号
sections.copy: Avoid tools that create the illusion of volume while hiding why the prospect matters. If every output looks generic, you will still spend time rewriting notes and guessing at relevance.
items: 没有可见的源注释或页面引用。
拟合得分和数据置信度之间没有区别。
No practical handoff into sheets or outreach tools.

example.title: 评估清单
example.copy: Before adopting a prospect research tool, run five real websites through it and compare whether the output helps you decide who to contact first.
items: Can the tool explain why the prospect fits your ICP?
Can you copy a credible first line without editing from scratch?
导出的字段能否保留外展角度背后的证据？

faqs.question: Should I use a lead database or a research tool?
faqs.answer: Use a database when you only need contact discovery. Use a research tool when the bottleneck is knowing which prospects deserve outreach and what to say.

faqs.question: 潜客卡片应包含多少个字段？
faqs.answer: Enough to preserve context: company, URL, fit score, confidence, signals, first line, contact path, source notes, status, and export status.

faqs.question: 人工智能生成的研究是否需要人工审查？
faqs.answer: Yes. AI should reduce research time, but you should review source notes before sending outreach or saving a prospect as qualified.

zh.title: 基于客户官网的开发信个性化开场白
zh.seoTitle: 客户官网开发信个性化开场白 | LeadCue
zh.description: 学习如何从客户官网观察点写出自然可信的开发信开场白，避免泛泛夸奖和硬套模板。
zh.intent: 教个人用户从客户官网证据中写出自然可信的开发信开场白。
zh.primaryKeyword: 客户官网开发信个性化
heroBullets: 开发信开场白要来自客户官网的真实观察，而不是泛泛夸奖。
第一句话要短、具体，并且能自然接到你的服务价值。
每个开场白都要保留来源备注，方便发送前复核。

sections.title: 好开场白为什么有效
sections.copy: 好的开发信开场白能让对方感觉你真的看过他们的官网，但不会显得过度熟络。它应该点出一个对方能马上验证的细节，再把这个细节连接到一个合理的业务结果。
items: 引用具体页面或模块，而不是写“你们网站很棒”。
不要夸大问题，只说官网上能看出来的事实。
让下一句话自然过渡到你的服务或建议。

sections.title: 从官网信号生成开场白
sections.copy: 先选一个最容易验证的官网信号，例如 CTA 太晚出现、案例入口不明显、博客长时间未更新或服务页没有回答关键问题。然后用一句话描述观察，再用一句话说明可能的影响。
items: CTA 信号适合网站建设、CRO 和增长服务商。
内容更新和服务页深度适合 SEO 服务商。
案例、评价和行业证明适合品牌、转化和销售支持类服务。

sections.title: 避免模板味
sections.copy: 模板可以提高效率，但不能让每封邮件看起来一样。最安全的做法是固定结构，不固定内容：观察点、影响、轻量建议可以复用框架，但每个客户的官网细节必须不同。
items: 把公司名称、页面位置和具体观察点换成真实信息。
删除任何无法从官网验证的判断。
不要在第一封邮件里塞完整方案，只给一个容易回复的小下一步。

example.title: 开发信开场白示例
example.copy: 我注意到你们首页把产品说明讲得很清楚，但预约演示入口要下滑后才出现。
items: 下一句：这可能让已经理解产品的访客多走一步才进入预约路径。
轻量 CTA：如果你愿意，我可以发 3 个首屏 CTA 和证明模块的快速建议。
避免写法：我很喜欢你们的网站，所以想联系你们。

faqs.question: 开发信开场白一定要个性化吗？
faqs.answer: 结构可以模板化，但观察点必须个性化。否则邮件很容易被看成批量群发。

faqs.question: 开场白应该多长？
faqs.answer: 一到两句话即可。移动端能快速读完，同时让对方知道你引用的是他们官网里的具体信息。

faqs.question: 可以用 AI 自动生成吗？
faqs.answer: 可以用 AI 加速，但发送前需要人工检查来源备注，确保邮件里没有无法验证的判断。

zh.title: 销售线索筛选：开发前先判断客户是否值得跟进
zh.seoTitle: 销售线索筛选流程 | LeadCue
zh.description: 用网站证据、匹配评分和开发理由筛选线索，避免把不合适的客户直接推进 CSV 或开发信序列。
zh.intent: 帮助机构定义什么使潜在客户值得保存和导出。
heroBullets: Qualification should happen before a prospect enters personal outreach.
A saved prospect needs both ICP fit and a reason to reach out.
置信度很重要，因为薄页数据会使信号变弱。

sections.title: 将拟合与证据质量分开
sections.copy: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer. Confidence answers whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust the recommendation. Both are needed before you save the prospect.
items: 适合度包括行业、公司类型、服务需求和报价相关性。
置信度包括页面深度、源清晰度和信号特异性。
A high-fit prospect with weak evidence may need manual review before outreach.

sections.title: 定义您的保存阈值
sections.copy: Set a clear threshold for when a prospect is worth saving. Without one, it is easy to keep too many companies that have no usable outreach angle.
items: 当拟合得分很高并且至少有两个网站提示支持该角度时保存。
当契合度很强但置信度中等时进行审查。
当唯一可用的注释是通用的或与您的报价无关时跳过。

sections.title: Qualification matrix
sections.copy: A clear matrix keeps high-fit prospects with weak evidence out of outreach while preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: High fit and high confidence: save as a Prospect Card and prioritize outreach.
High fit and low confidence: review another page before export.
Low fit and strong signal: archive the note unless your offer or ICP changes.

sections.title: 将资质带入出口
sections.copy: The qualification context should not disappear when a prospect leaves the research tool. Export fields should include fit score, signal summary, first line, source notes, follow-up status, and saved/exported status.
items: Use status fields to prevent duplicate work.
Keep source notes in exports or your own notes for review.
Export only saved prospects when building outreach lists.

example.title: 实用的保存规则
example.copy: Save a prospect when it matches the ICP, has at least two source-backed website cues, and has a first line you would be comfortable sending.
items: 适合分数：80 或更高。
置信度：70% 或更高。
Status: saved, reviewed, and ready for CSV export.

faqs.question: 什么是良好拟合分数阈值？
faqs.answer: 从 80 开始进行高优先级外展，然后根据回复质量和预订的会议进行调整，而不仅仅是扫描量。

faqs.question: Should weak-fit prospects be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not always. Keep them out of immediate outreach, but use notes to understand whether the ICP or scoring rules need refinement.

faqs.question: 机构应如何审核资质质量？
faqs.answer: Review saved prospects weekly by fit score, confidence, reply quality, and whether the first line matched a real source note.

zh.title: 网站建设公司客户开发：从客户官网找到改版机会
zh.seoTitle: 网站建设公司客户开发方法 | LeadCue
zh.description: 帮助网站建设公司从客户官网的首屏、CTA、案例和联系路径中找到自然的改版沟通切入点。
zh.intent: 说明网站建设公司如何用客户官网证据筛选更值得开发的改版客户。
zh.primaryKeyword: 网站建设公司客户开发
heroBullets: 从首屏、视觉层级和 CTA 发现可验证的改版理由。
把官网问题转成低压力开发信，而不是一上来推销整站重做。
只保存有明确网站证据和沟通价值的客户。

sections.title: 网站建设公司最容易找到的信号
sections.copy: 网站建设类服务最适合从客户官网切入，因为买家能马上看到你提到的问题。首屏不清楚、CTA 不明显、案例隐藏太深、移动端体验弱，都是比泛泛说“需要改版”更可信的开发理由。
items: 首屏是否在几秒内说清服务、对象和下一步。
预约、咨询或报价入口是否足够明显。
案例、客户评价和结果证明是否在买家决策前出现。

sections.title: 把改版机会写成开发信
sections.copy: 开发信不要直接说“你们网站需要重做”。更自然的方式是引用一个具体观察，再说明它可能影响预约、信任或咨询路径，最后提出发送几个小建议。
items: 用一个页面观察作为开场白。
把观察点连接到转化或信任问题。
下一步只提出发送 3 个改进点，降低回复成本。

sections.title: 适合保存到线索卡的信息
sections.copy: 网站建设客户开发需要保留的不只是公司名。个人用户应该能看到官网证据、改版理由、开发信开场白和后续建议，这样 BD 或销售接手时不会重新研究一遍。
items: 官网 URL、页面位置和具体观察点。
改版机会类型：首屏、CTA、案例、移动端或联系路径。
适合发送的第一句话和建议角度。

example.title: 网站建设开发信切入示例
example.copy: 我注意到你们首页的服务说明很完整，但预约咨询入口在首屏里不太明显，买家需要继续下滑才能知道下一步怎么联系。
items: 信号：首屏 CTA 弱。
业务角度：已经感兴趣的访客可能没有立即行动入口。
下一步：发送 3 个首屏 CTA 和案例展示的小调整建议。

faqs.question: 网站建设公司应该开发哪些客户？
faqs.answer: 优先开发官网上有明显转化、信任或信息结构问题，同时业务本身有预算和更新需求的公司。

faqs.question: 开发信里要不要直接说对方网站不好？
faqs.answer: 不建议。最好引用一个具体、可验证的观察点，并把语气放在“可以优化的机会”上。

faqs.question: 如何避免像模板群发？
faqs.answer: 每封邮件至少替换真实页面位置、观察点和建议方向，让对方能确认你真的看过官网。

zh.title: SEO 客户开发：从网站内容缺口找到沟通理由
zh.seoTitle: SEO客户开发工具与网站线索筛选 | LeadCue
zh.description: 帮助 SEO 服务商从客户官网的内容更新、服务页深度和搜索意图缺口中筛选更适合开发的客户。
zh.intent: 说明 SEO 服务商如何用网站证据判断哪些客户值得外联。
secondaryKeywords: 网站内容诊断客户开发
heroBullets: 用内容更新、服务页深度和内部链接发现 SEO 开发机会。
把官网缺口写成有帮助的外联建议，而不是泛泛推 SEO 服务。
为每个客户保留来源备注，方便后续复核。

sections.title: SEO 客户开发适合看哪些信号
sections.copy: SEO 服务商不应该只看行业和公司规模。客户官网本身会暴露很多需求信号，比如博客长期未更新、服务页只写了几句、案例没有覆盖核心行业，或者买家常搜的问题没有对应内容。
items: 内容新鲜度：博客、资源页或案例是否还在更新。
服务页深度：是否回答了买家的具体疑问。
搜索意图覆盖：是否缺少高意图主题、比较页或场景页。

sections.title: 从 SEO 缺口到开发信角度
sections.copy: 好的 SEO 开发信不要直接说“你们需要 SEO”。更好的做法是指出一个可见内容缺口，再说明它可能让有需求的买家在搜索阶段流失。
items: 先引用具体页面或内容类型。
说明这个缺口和搜索需求、询盘或演示预约的关系。
提出发送几个优先级最高的内容角度。

sections.title: 给个人用户的交接字段
sections.copy: SEO 客户开发的后续跟进通常需要更多上下文。保存内容缺口、相关页面、建议主题和第一句话，可以让销售或顾问更快进入有效对话。
items: 主要内容缺口和对应页面。
建议优先测试的关键词/主题方向。
可复制的开发信开场白和来源备注。

example.title: SEO 开发信切入示例
example.copy: 我看到你们服务页把核心能力写清楚了，但博客和资源页最近更新较少，像“财务个人用户如何评估报告工具”这类高意图问题还没有对应内容。
items: 信号：内容更新和买家问题覆盖不足。
业务角度：搜索阶段的高意图需求可能没有被承接。
下一步：发送 3 个可以优先测试的内容主题。

faqs.question: SEO 客户开发应该先看排名数据还是官网？
faqs.answer: 两者都重要，但冷启动时官网更容易快速判断需求信号。之后再用关键词和排名数据验证机会大小。

faqs.question: 开发信里可以提关键词吗？
faqs.answer: 可以，但不要堆砌。最好只提一两个和业务高度相关的主题，并说明为什么它们值得测试。

faqs.question: 哪些 SEO 线索不值得保存？
faqs.answer: 如果官网内容已经很完整、业务与服务不匹配，或没有明确可沟通缺口，就不应该进入高优先级外联。

zh.title: 营销服务公司客户开发：从网站转化路径找到切入点
zh.seoTitle: 营销服务公司客户开发流程 | LeadCue
zh.description: 帮助营销和增长服务商从客户官网的活动入口、信任证明和转化路径中找到更自然的开发理由。
zh.intent: 说明营销服务公司如何用客户官网证据筛选和个性化开发线索。
zh.primaryKeyword: 营销服务公司客户开发
heroBullets: 从活动入口、落地页和证明模块发现营销服务机会。
把网站转化摩擦写成具体建议，而不是泛泛说要投放或增长。
让研究、第一句话和 CSV 导出字段保持一致。

sections.title: 营销服务适合看哪些官网信号
sections.copy: 营销服务的开发机会通常藏在转化路径里。比如广告流量落地页缺少信任证明，主 CTA 不清楚，案例和结果数据没有支撑主张，或者内容与目标买家的痛点不匹配。
items: 活动入口和落地页是否承接清楚。
首屏是否说明目标买家、结果和下一步。
客户证明、案例和数据是否足够靠前。

sections.title: 从观察点到增长建议
sections.copy: 营销开发信的关键是避免空泛。不要只说“我们帮你增长”。先指出网站上的一个转化摩擦，再提出一个小的测试方向，例如首屏文案、证明模块或 CTA 路径。
items: 用具体页面观察建立可信度。
把观察点连接到转化率、获客成本或预约率。
下一步建议一个小测试，而不是完整方案。

sections.title: 让后续跟进更顺
sections.copy: 营销服务通常需要多人协作。把官网证据、建议测试、第一句话和导出字段放在同一张线索卡里，可以让销售、策略和执行个人用户共享同一个判断依据。
items: 保存转化摩擦和来源页面。
标记适合的服务方向：落地页、投放、内容或 CRO。
导出到 CSV 时保留建议角度和来源备注。

example.title: 营销服务开发信切入示例
example.copy: 我看到你们首页清楚介绍了产品，但客户案例和量化结果在首屏里不明显，付费或外联流量进入后可能需要更多信任信息才会预约。
items: 信号：证明模块出现较晚。
业务角度：高意图访客可能在预约前缺少信任支撑。
下一步：发送 3 个首屏证明和 CTA 路径的小测试建议。

faqs.question: 营销服务公司应该优先找什么类型客户？
faqs.answer: 优先找有明确产品或服务、正在获客但官网转化路径和信任证明不足的客户。

faqs.question: 开发信要不要提广告或预算？
faqs.answer: 第一封邮件不一定要提预算。先用网站观察建立可信度，再在后续对话里讨论渠道和投入。

faqs.question: 如何判断这个客户适合增长服务？
faqs.answer: 看是否有清晰业务、可见转化路径、足够客单价或销售价值，以及官网上可改善的获客摩擦。

zh.title: 如何把客户官网转成开发信切入角度
zh.seoTitle: 客户官网开发信切入角度指南 | LeadCue
zh.description: 把官网观察点转成开发信的第一句话、业务影响和低压力下一步，让外联更像真实研究。
zh.intent: 为操作员提供可重复的工作流程，用于从网站构建外展角度。
zh.primaryKeyword: 客户官网开发信切入角度
heroBullets: 扫描网站以获取与业务相关的具体观察结果。
将观察结果与可能的买家结果联系起来。
提供一个小的下一步，使回复变得容易。

sections.title: 第 1 步：选择正确的页面
sections.copy: 从主页、定价页面、产品页面、服务页面或主导航开始。这些页面通常会显示最强烈的业务信号，而无需进行全面审核。
items: 查看标题、CTA、证明、导航和转化路径。
寻找买家的摩擦，而不是次要的设计偏好。
捕获源代码，以便稍后检查注释。

sections.title: 步骤 2：翻译观察结果
sections.copy: 当网站观察解释了可能的业务影响时，它就变成了外展角度。我们的目标不是诊断一切；而是诊断一切。这是为了创造一个可信的理由来开始对话。
items: 观察：演示 CTA 位于第一个滚动条下方。
可能的影响：高意向访客可能不会在注意力最高时采取行动。
提供：发送三个英雄部分的想法。

sections.title: 步骤 3：写电子邮件
sections.copy: 保持电子邮件紧凑。使用第一行证明相关性，第二句解释机会，并使用 CTA 请求允许发送想法。
items: 不要夸大光扫描的确定性。
不要在第一次接触时推销每项服务。
让回复变得简单：“想要我把它们寄过去吗？”

example.title: 冷电子邮件角度示例
example.copy: 我注意到产品故事很清楚，但财务买家证明和演示 CTA 在第一个滚动后出现。这可能会花费已经了解该产品的访问者预订演示的费用。
items: 第一行：具体且可验证。
机会：与预订的演示相关。
CTA：发送三个快速修复。

faqs.question: 一封电子邮件应提及多少个网站信号？
faqs.answer: 通常一个强信号就足够了。提及太多信号可能会让电子邮件感觉像是审计转储。

faqs.question: CTA 是否应该要求召开会议？
faqs.answer: 对于冷的初次接触，较轻的 CTA 通常效果更好。在要求会面之前主动提出想法、拆解或示例。

faqs.question: 如果网站没有明显问题怎么办？
faqs.answer: Do not force it. Either scan another page, save the prospect for later review, or skip it until you have a stronger reason to reach out.

zh.title: 客户网站开发筛选清单：外联前先评分
zh.seoTitle: 客户网站开发筛选清单 | LeadCue
zh.description: 用一套轻量清单检查客户官网是否有明确开发理由，再决定是否保存、分配或导出线索。
zh.intent: Give solo operators a scoring method they can use before saving and exporting prospects.
zh.primaryKeyword: 客户网站开发筛选清单
heroBullets: Fit score tells you whether the prospect matches your offer.
信号质量告诉您外展原因是否充分。
Export readiness tells you whether the prospect is ready for the next step.

sections.title: 评分 ICP 首先适合
sections.copy: Start with the company itself. If it does not match your offer focus, even a clear website gap may not be worth pursuing.
items: 行业和市场契合度。
公司规模或复杂性适合。
提供网页设计、SEO、营销或增长服务的相关性。

sections.title: 对网站提示进行评分
sections.copy: 网站提示应该足够具体以支持第一行，并且足够有意义以暗示商机。弱线索是通用的、主观的或与你的报价无关的。
items: 强烈提示：演示 CTA 在首屏上不可见。
中等提示：博客似乎处于非活动状态，但来源日期不清楚。
弱提示：网站可以看起来更现代。

sections.title: Weighted scoring matrix
sections.copy: A weighted model gives you the same decision language each time and makes it easier to tune the workflow after outreach runs.
