items: ICP fit: 40 points for industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
Opportunity signal: 35 points for source specificity, business implication, and offer relevance.
Readiness: 25 points for confidence, contact path, first line, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: 出口准备度分数
sections.copy: A prospect is not ready just because it has a high score. The handoff fields need to be complete enough for you to use without redoing the research.
items: 第一行已准备好复制。
附有源注释。
Follow-up status and export fields are set.

example.title: 简单评分模型
example.copy: 使用 40 分表示 ICP 拟合度，35 分表示网站提示，15 分表示信心，10 分表示联系路径和出口准备情况。
items: 80-100：保存并确定优先级。
65-79：在外展之前手动审核。
65 以下：跳过或稍后再看。

faqs.question: 评分应该完全自动化吗？
faqs.answer: Automation should create a recommendation, but you should review edge cases and use reply quality to tune the model.

faqs.question: 适合度和置信度之间有什么区别？
faqs.answer: Fit measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures how strong and complete the available evidence is.

faqs.question: 是否应该导出低分潜在客户？
faqs.answer: Usually no. Exporting weak prospects creates downstream work and lowers outreach quality.

zh.title: 网站诊断客户开发：先给有用建议，再约沟通
zh.seoTitle: 网站诊断客户开发指南 | LeadCue
zh.description: 用网站诊断式外联提供具体建议，而不是一上来推销服务，适合网站建设、SEO 和增长服务商。
zh.intent: 说明如何把轻量网站诊断转成更容易获得回复的客户开发邮件。
heroBullets: 先给客户一个可验证的网站观察，而不是直接推销服务。
把网站诊断控制在轻量范围，让对方愿意回复。
适合网站建设、SEO、CRO 和增长服务商做冷启动开发。

sections.title: 网站诊断式客户开发的核心
sections.copy: 网站诊断式开发不是免费做完整审计，而是用官网上最明显、最容易验证的一两个信号，开启一段有用的商业对话。重点是让对方感觉你带来了具体观察，而不是又一封模板推销。
items: 只选最强的观察点，不要罗列十几个问题。
把观察点连接到转化、信任、搜索或预约路径。
下一步提出发送简短建议，而不是马上要求开会。

sections.title: 适合写进第一封邮件的诊断点
sections.copy: 第一封邮件里的诊断点要足够具体，也要足够轻。比如首屏 CTA 不明显、案例入口隐藏、服务页没有回答买家疑问、内容长期未更新，这些都比泛泛说“你们网站需要优化”更容易获得回复。
items: 首屏和 CTA：适合网站建设与转化优化切入。
案例和证明：适合信任建设、落地页和销售支持切入。
内容和服务页：适合 SEO、内容策略和需求捕获切入。

sections.title: 把诊断变成低压力 CTA
sections.copy: 最好的下一步不是“约个 30 分钟会议”，而是“我可以发 3 个具体建议”。这样对方的回复成本更低，也能让你在后续邮件里展示专业度。
items: 用“我可以发你几个想法”替代“我们聊聊”。
不要在第一封邮件附上长篇审计报告。
保留来源备注，后续跟进时可以继续引用同一条官网证据。

example.title: 网站诊断开发信示例
example.copy: 我看到你们首页把产品价值讲得很清楚，但客户案例入口在主导航里不太明显，强意图买家可能需要多找几步才能看到证明。
items: 切入点：信任证明路径不够直接。
业务影响：买家在预约前可能缺少足够的风险缓释信息。
下一步：我可以发 3 个更容易露出案例和预约入口的小调整。

faqs.question: 网站诊断式开发会不会像免费咨询？
faqs.answer: 不会，只要范围控制得足够小。第一封邮件只需要一个观察点和一个轻量建议，不需要完整方案。

faqs.question: 适合给所有客户都发吗？
faqs.answer: 不适合。只有当官网上确实有可验证的改进点，而且与你的服务强相关时，才值得使用这种方式。

faqs.question: 是否需要附截图？
faqs.answer: 可以在后续邮件或回复后再发。第一封邮件通常保持简短更好，避免附件降低送达和阅读意愿。

ja.title: 見込み客リサーチツール：Webサイトから営業すべき企業を選ぶ
ja.seoTitle: 見込み客リサーチツール | LeadCue
ja.description: WebサイトのCTA、実績、コンテンツ、問い合わせ導線から、個人プロフェッショナルが営業すべき見込み客とアプローチ理由を見つけます。
ja.intent: Webサイトを起点に、営業すべき見込み客を選ぶ方法を説明します。
ja.primaryKeyword: 見込み客リサーチツール
secondaryKeywords: 営業前 Webサイト チェックリスト
heroBullets: 一般的なリードリストではなく、見込み客のWebサイトから判断を始めます。
ファーストビュー、実績、CTA、コンテンツ更新、問い合わせ導線から営業すべき理由を見つけます。
観察した根拠を、営業メールの冒頭文、提案角度、CSV向けメモに変換します。

sections.title: Webサイト起点の見込み客リサーチとは
sections.copy: Webサイト起点の見込み客リサーチは、連絡先を増やすための作業ではありません。営業する前に相手のWebサイトを確認し、自社サービスと合うか、自然に連絡できる理由があるかを見極めるためのプロセスです。
items: Web制作、SEO、CRO、グロース支援、メッセージ改善の個人プロフェッショナルに向いています。
目的はリード数を増やすことではなく、営業すべき企業を絞ることです。
よい出力は、なぜ今連絡する価値があるのかを説明できます。

sections.title: 見るべきWebサイトのシグナル
sections.copy: 価値の高いシグナルは、単なる見た目の感想ではなく、買い手の行動や事業成果につながるものです。CTAが見つけにくい、実績が深い階層にある、サービスページが薄いといった点は、営業の切り口になりやすいです。
items: ファーストビュー：見出し、価値提案、CTA、信頼要素が明確か。
信頼材料：事例、レビュー、ロゴ、成果データが見つけやすいか。
コンテンツと導線：更新頻度、サービスページの深さ、問い合わせ経路が十分か。

sections.title: 営業メールの文脈へ変える
sections.copy: 営業メールでは「サイトを拝見しました」だけでは弱すぎます。見たこと、起こり得る影響、軽い提案を短くつなげると、テンプレートではなく実際のリサーチに見えます。
items: 冒頭文では具体的なページ上の観察を1つだけ書きます。
次の文で、転換率、信頼、SEO、問い合わせ導線への影響を説明します。
最初の依頼は軽くし、3つの改善案を送る程度に留めます。

example.title: 見込み客リサーチメモの例
example.copy: Northstar Analytics は製品説明が明確ですが、デモ予約CTAと財務個人ユーザー向けの実績がファーストビューには見えていません。
items: シグナル：デモ予約の導線が下部にあり、強い関心を持つ訪問者が一手間かかります。
営業角度：製品理解は進んでも、すぐに行動する理由が弱くなる可能性があります。
提案：ファーストビューのCTA、実績ブロック、事例導線について3つの改善案を送る。

faqs.question: Webサイト起点のリサーチはスクレイピングですか？
faqs.answer: いいえ。公開されているWebサイト情報を確認し、営業判断とメール文脈を作るための作業です。連絡先データベースの購入やSNSスクレイピングとは異なります。

faqs.question: どんな個人ユーザーに向いていますか？
faqs.answer: Web制作、SEO、CRO、グロース支援、メッセージ改善など、Webサイト上の課題と自社サービスを結び付けられる個人ユーザーに向いています。

faqs.question: CSVには何を残すべきですか？
faqs.answer: 会社名、URL、適合スコア、主なWebサイトシグナル、ソースメモ、営業メールの冒頭文、フォロー状況、ステージ、エクスポート状態を残すと引き継ぎやすくなります。

ja.title: AI website prospecting tool: qualify prospects before outreach
ja.seoTitle: AI Website Prospecting Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
ja.description: Use AI website prospecting to find opportunity signals, score prospect fit, and prepare source-backed first lines before outreach.
ja.intent: Define the LeadCue method for solo professionals who research prospect websites before outreach.
heroBullets: Start with what the prospect's website already proves instead of a generic contact record.
Use opportunity signals and fit scoring to decide whether the prospect deserves outreach.
Turn the research into a Prospect Card that carries context into CSV or email workflows.

sections.title: What AI website prospecting changes
sections.copy: Traditional outbound often starts with a list and searches for a contact. AI website prospecting starts with the company website and asks whether there is a credible reason to contact the prospect at all.
items: The first decision is qualification, not email discovery.
The research output is a source-backed reason to contact, not a vague personalization line.
The handoff keeps website evidence, fit score, first line, and export fields together.

sections.title: Workflow matrix
sections.copy: The workflow should move from public evidence to prospect judgment to outreach preparation without losing context between tools.
items: Review: inspect homepage, proof, CTA, service pages, content freshness, and contact path.
Qualify: score ICP fit, signal strength, confidence, and outreach readiness.
Prepare: save a Prospect Card with source notes, first line, outreach angle, follow-up status, and export status.

sections.title: When it is the right motion
sections.copy: Website prospecting is strongest when your offer can be connected to visible website improvements or missed opportunities.
items: Web design freelancers can use CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion signals.
SEO consultants can use thin pages, stale content, weak internal paths, and missing intent pages.
Growth consultants can use positioning, campaign path, lead magnet, and proof gaps.

example.title: Website-first research example
example.copy: Instead of saving a SaaS company because it matches an industry filter, you save it because the site has a clear product story, hidden finance proof, and no above-the-fold demo CTA.
items: Signal: proof and CTA are not visible when intent is highest.
Fit: your offer is conversion-focused website improvements.
Output: a Prospect Card with a verifiable first line and export-ready note.

faqs.question: Is AI website prospecting the same as scraping?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow uses visible public website evidence, human review, and source-backed notes. It is not designed to scrape social platforms or build hidden contact intelligence.

faqs.question: Who should use AI website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for SEO consultants, web design freelancers, CRO specialists, growth consultants, positioning consultants, and solo founders because their offers can be tied to website evidence.

faqs.question: What should be produced before outreach starts?
faqs.answer: A saved Prospect Card should include fit score, opportunity signals, source notes, first line, reason to contact, and export status.

ja.title: AI prospect research tool: the step before cold email
ja.seoTitle: AI Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
ja.description: Learn why solo professionals need a prospect research tool that qualifies companies, preserves source evidence, and prepares outreach context before sending cold email.
ja.intent: Position LeadCue as the research layer between prospect lists and outreach execution.
secondaryKeywords: 認定されたアウトリーチ ワークフロー
heroBullets: A spreadsheet stores prospects after they are accepted; a research layer decides whether they should be accepted.
An email tool sends messages; a research layer prepares the source-backed reason to send one.
A website research layer keeps Prospect Cards, fit scores, and opportunity signals together.

sections.title: Where the research layer sits
sections.copy: Many solo professionals have contact sources, spreadsheets, and email tools, but no dedicated place to decide whether a website-backed prospect is worth outreach.
items: Before export: qualify the website and avoid adding weak prospects.
Before email: prepare first lines and context that can be verified.
Before follow-up: preserve why the prospect was selected, skipped, or reviewed.

sections.title: Decision table for tool boundaries
sections.copy: A focused research layer should not pretend to replace every outbound tool. Its value is to improve the quality of prospects and context handed downstream.
items: Contact database: finds people or emails; research layer explains why the company is worth contacting.
Pipeline tool: manages follow-up status; research layer creates the source-backed context before export.
Email sequencer: sends campaigns; research layer prepares claims, first lines, and notes before sending.

sections.title: Signals the layer should preserve
sections.copy: The layer becomes useful when every saved prospect carries enough context for you to understand the decision later without repeating the research.
items: Website evidence: page observation, source path, and buyer implication.
Qualification: ICP fit, confidence, save threshold, and opportunity category.
Handoff: first line, short email context, export fields, status, and follow-up notes.

example.title: Research layer handoff example
example.copy: You review a website, save a Prospect Card, and export only the qualified prospect with fit score, source notes, first line, and a short outreach angle attached.
items: The exported row receives context, not just a URL.
The email writer can verify the claim before sending.
You can review why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Does a website research layer replace an outreach tool?
faqs.answer: No. It sits before your outreach workflow and prepares qualified, source-backed prospect context for export or handoff.

faqs.question: Does it replace an email finder?
faqs.answer: No. Email finders help locate contacts. A website research layer helps explain whether the prospect is worth contacting and what the outreach should reference.

faqs.question: What makes the layer useful for solo professionals?
faqs.answer: Solo professionals can connect their offer to visible website opportunities, then save that evidence as a reusable Prospect Card.

ja.title: Website opportunity signals solo professionals can use before outreach
ja.seoTitle: Website Opportunity Signals for Solo Outreach | LeadCue
ja.description: Use website opportunity signals to spot CTA, proof, SEO, content, positioning, and timing cues that can support source-backed personal outreach.
ja.intent: Give solo professionals a signal taxonomy for turning website observations into outreach reasons.
secondaryKeywords: Webサイトの販売シグナル
heroBullets: Opportunity signals connect a visible page observation to a business-relevant outreach angle.
The best signals are specific enough for a buyer to verify and relevant enough for your offer.
Weak signals should trigger review or skip decisions instead of automatic export.

sections.title: Signal taxonomy
sections.copy: A useful taxonomy keeps you from treating subjective design opinions as sales evidence. Each signal should map to a buyer outcome and your offer.
items: Conversion signals: hidden CTA, unclear next step, confusing navigation, weak contact path.
Trust signals: missing case studies, buried proof, no results, weak testimonials, unclear customer segments.
Content signals: stale blog, thin service pages, missing use cases, weak internal linking, outdated resources.

sections.title: How to score signal strength
sections.copy: Signal strength depends on visibility, relevance, and confidence. A visible issue that matches your offer is stronger than a broad guess about the business.
items: Strong: the source is visible, the implication is clear, and the offer can help.
Medium: the pattern is plausible but needs another source or manual review.
Weak: the note is subjective, generic, or disconnected from your offer.

sections.title: Turn signals into outreach context
sections.copy: Signals become useful when they are converted into a reason to contact, a first line, and a Prospect Card note you can verify later.
items: Observation: what the page shows and where it appears.
Implication: why it may affect conversion, trust, discovery, or buyer confidence.
Next step: a low-pressure suggestion such as sending three ideas or a short teardown.

example.title: Opportunity signal example
example.copy: The product page is clear, but the strongest customer proof appears below the main CTA and the navigation does not surface industry-specific examples.
items: Category: trust proof and conversion path.
Reason to contact: buyers may need proof earlier before booking.
First line: mention the proof placement and offer a small set of ideas.

faqs.question: How many opportunity signals should a prospect need?
faqs.answer: One strong signal can be enough for a first touch. Two or three source-backed signals make qualification more reliable.

faqs.question: Are opportunity signals the same as a full audit?
faqs.answer: No. They are lightweight sales research cues used before outreach. A full audit can happen after the prospect engages.

faqs.question: What should happen when the signal is weak?
faqs.answer: Do not export the prospect automatically. Mark it for review, scan another page, or skip until a stronger reason to contact appears.

ja.title: How to find a reason to contact a prospect from their website
ja.seoTitle: Find a Reason to Contact Prospects from Website Evidence | LeadCue
ja.description: Find a credible reason to contact a prospect by reviewing website signals, qualifying fit, and turning source evidence into a useful first line.
ja.intent: Help solo operators solve the core problem of why this prospect should be contacted now.
heroBullets: A good reason to contact is specific, visible, relevant to the offer, and easy to verify.
The website should provide evidence, not just a name to personalize around.
If there is no credible reason, the prospect should be skipped or reviewed instead of exported.

sections.title: A simple qualification order
sections.copy: Start with prospect fit, then inspect the website for evidence, then decide whether the observation supports a useful first touch.
items: Fit: does the company match your ICP, offer, and target market?
Evidence: does the website show a specific opportunity signal or gap?
Message: can the observation become a first line and next step without overclaiming?

sections.title: Reason-to-contact checklist
sections.copy: Before saving the prospect, you should be able to answer five questions that make the outreach defensible.
items: What did we see on the website, and where did we see it?
Why might it matter to the buyer?
Why is your offer relevant to that observation?

sections.title: Bad reasons to avoid
sections.copy: Weak reasons make outreach feel automated even when a first line is technically personalized. The problem is not word choice; it is lack of relevance.
items: Vague compliments such as liking the website without a specific observation.
Unverified claims about performance, rankings, revenue, or conversion impact.
Signals that do not connect to your offer or buyer outcome.

example.title: Reason to contact example
example.copy: The website targets finance buyers, but the homepage does not surface finance proof or a clear demo CTA above the first scroll.
items: Observation: proof and CTA placement are visible website details.
Implication: high-intent visitors may not see enough trust before acting.
Next step: offer three hero and proof-section ideas.

faqs.question: What if a website has no obvious issue?
faqs.answer: Do not force a reason. Scan another important page, review a different offer angle, or skip the prospect until stronger evidence appears.

faqs.question: Should the first email include every reason?
faqs.answer: No. Usually one strong reason is enough. Too many observations can make a first touch feel like an unsolicited audit.

faqs.question: Can a reason to contact be positive?
faqs.answer: Yes, if it is specific and relevant. The reason can be an opportunity to build on a strength, not only a problem to fix.

ja.category: ソースに裏付けられたメモ
ja.title: Source-backed prospect notes for safer personal outreach
ja.seoTitle: Source-Backed Prospect Notes for Personal Outreach | LeadCue
ja.description: Create source-backed prospect notes that keep website evidence, uncertainty, first lines, and export context attached to each saved prospect.
ja.intent: Show solo professionals how to preserve evidence so outreach copy can be reviewed before sending.
heroBullets: Source-backed notes help you verify claims before they appear in outreach.
They reduce generic personalization by tying each first line to a visible page observation.
They make export safer because context travels with the prospect.

sections.title: What source-backed means
sections.copy: A source-backed note states what was observed, where it was observed, why it may matter, and how confident you should be before using it in outreach.
items: Source: page type, URL path, section, or visible website area.
Observation: the exact cue, not a broad interpretation.
Confidence: whether the evidence is strong, incomplete, or needs manual review.

sections.title: Note structure
sections.copy: The best notes are compact enough for export but complete enough that you can understand and verify the outreach angle later.
items: Observed cue: demo CTA appears below the first scroll.
Possible implication: visitors may need a clearer next step when intent is highest.
Outbound use: ask permission to send three hero CTA and proof-section ideas.

sections.title: Review before sending
sections.copy: Source-backed notes should improve judgment, not remove it. Review the source and remove any claim that cannot be supported.
items: Avoid revenue, ranking, or conversion claims without evidence.
Keep uncertainty visible when a signal is incomplete.
Use the note as context, not as a final message that must be sent unchanged.

example.title: Source-backed note example
example.copy: Homepage review: the product story is clear, but the finance proof and demo CTA appear after the first scroll. Possible angle: make buyer trust and next step visible earlier.
items: Specific source: homepage first viewport.
Clear implication: proof and next step visibility.
Safe use: offer ideas without claiming confirmed conversion loss.

faqs.question: Should source notes be exported?
faqs.answer: Yes. Put short source notes in export fields or notes so you can verify why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Can AI-generated notes be used directly?
faqs.answer: They should be reviewed first. The note must match visible evidence before it becomes outreach copy.

faqs.question: How long should a source-backed note be?
faqs.answer: Long enough to preserve the observation, implication, and source, but short enough to scan during outreach review.

ja.category: フィットスコアリング
ja.title: ICP fit score for solo website prospecting
ja.seoTitle: ICP Fit Score for Solo Website Prospecting | LeadCue
ja.description: Build an ICP fit score that combines prospect fit, website opportunity signals, evidence confidence, and export readiness before personal outreach.
ja.intent: Help solo professionals separate prospect fit from signal confidence and outreach readiness.
secondaryKeywords: 見込み顧客適合スコア
heroBullets: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer.
Confidence score answers whether the website evidence is strong enough to use.
Export readiness answers whether the prospect can move downstream without rework.

sections.title: Fit score model
sections.copy: A practical score should combine the prospect profile with website evidence and handoff readiness instead of treating all signals as equal.
items: 40 points: ICP match across industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
35 points: opportunity signal quality, source specificity, and buyer implication.
25 points: confidence, contact path, first-line readiness, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: Decision thresholds
sections.copy: Thresholds help you avoid exporting weak prospects while still preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: 80-100: save and prioritize for outreach after source review.
65-79: review manually or scan another page before export.
Below 65: skip, archive, or revisit when the ICP or offer changes.

sections.title: Common scoring mistakes
sections.copy: A high fit score is only useful when the evidence is strong enough to support a real outreach reason.
items: Giving high scores to prospects that match the industry but have no visible reason to contact.
Mixing prospect fit with confidence so weak evidence looks stronger than it is.
Exporting prospects before first line, source notes, and export fields are complete.

example.title: Fit score example
example.copy: A B2B SaaS company matches your ICP, shows a hidden demo CTA and missing proof path, and has enough source evidence for a first line.
items: ICP fit: 36/40.
Signal quality: 28/35.
Readiness: 21/25. Total: 85, save after review.

faqs.question: Is fit score the same as confidence score?
faqs.answer: No. Fit score measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust.

faqs.question: What score should qualify a lead?
faqs.answer: Start with 80 for high-priority outreach, then adjust based on reply quality, booked meetings, and manual QA results.

faqs.question: Should low-score leads be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Keep them out of outreach, but use their notes to improve ICP rules or review later.

ja.intent: Help buyers evaluate prospect research software for a personal workflow.
ja.primaryKeyword: 個人プロフェッショナル向け見込み客リサーチツール
secondaryKeywords: アウトバウンド営業 リサーチ
heroBullets: 調査ツールは、リードの量を増やすだけでなく、リードの質を向上させる必要があります。
出力では、見込み顧客に連絡する価値がある理由を説明する必要があります。
Exports should carry source notes into the tools you already use.

sections.title: ツールが実行すべきジョブ
sections.copy: A prospect research tool should help you decide whether a company deserves outreach. That means it needs to combine ICP fit, website evidence, outreach angle, and handoff fields instead of only collecting company names.
items: Qualify prospects before they enter outreach.
各販売キューの背後にあるソースを表示します。
実際のページの観察を参照するコピーを作成します。

sections.title: 重要な機能
sections.copy: The most useful features sit between research and outreach. They help the operator move from a website scan to a saved prospect card, then to a CSV row without losing context.
items: 優先順位付けのための適合スコアと信頼スコア。
Web デザイン、SEO、マーケティング、タイミングごとにグループ化された Webサイトの合図。
Export fields that map to a spreadsheet, email workflow, or custom template.

sections.title: Tool evaluation matrix
sections.copy: When comparing prospect research tools, separate contact discovery from website-backed qualification so you do not buy a list tool for a research-quality problem.
items: Contact discovery: useful when the bottleneck is finding people or emails.
Website research layer: useful when the bottleneck is deciding why the prospect is worth contacting.
Outbound handoff: useful when you need first lines, source notes, fit score, and export-ready fields together.

sections.title: ツールを比較する際の危険信号
sections.copy: Avoid tools that create the illusion of volume while hiding why the prospect matters. If every output looks generic, you will still spend time rewriting notes and guessing at relevance.
items: ソース ノートや参照ページは表示されません。
適合スコアとデータの信頼性の間には区別がありません。
No practical handoff into sheets or outreach tools.

example.title: 評価チェックリスト
example.copy: Before adopting a prospect research tool, run five real websites through it and compare whether the output helps you decide who to contact first.
items: Can the tool explain why the prospect fits your ICP?
Can you copy a credible first line without editing from scratch?
エクスポートされたフィールドは、アウトリーチ アングルの背後にある証拠を保存できますか?

faqs.question: Should I use a lead database or a research tool?
faqs.answer: Use a database when you only need contact discovery. Use a research tool when the bottleneck is knowing which prospects deserve outreach and what to say.

faqs.question: プロスペクトカードにはフィールドをいくつ含める必要がありますか?
faqs.answer: Enough to preserve context: company, URL, fit score, confidence, signals, first line, contact path, source notes, status, and export status.

faqs.question: AI によって生成された研究には人間によるレビューが必要ですか?
faqs.answer: Yes. AI should reduce research time, but you should review source notes before sending outreach or saving a prospect as qualified.

ja.title: Webサイトの根拠から作る営業メールの書き出し
ja.seoTitle: 営業メール 書き出し 例文 | LeadCue
ja.description: 見込み客のWebサイトで見つけた具体的な観察点から、自然で信頼できる営業メールの書き出しを作ります。
ja.intent: 見込み客サイトの根拠から、自然で信頼できる営業メール冒頭文を作る方法を示します。
ja.primaryKeyword: 営業メール 書き出し 例文
secondaryKeywords: 営業メール パーソナライズ
コールドメール 冒頭文
Webサイト根拠 メール
heroBullets: 営業メールの冒頭文は、相手のWebサイトで確認できる事実から作ります。
短く、具体的で、自社の提案につながる内容にします。
送信前に確認できるよう、元になったページのメモを残します。

sections.title: よい冒頭文の条件
sections.copy: よい冒頭文は、相手に「ちゃんと見てくれた」と感じさせます。ただし距離感を間違える必要はありません。確認できる事実を短く書き、それがなぜ商談や改善につながるのかを自然に示します。
items: 曖昧な褒め言葉ではなく、具体的なページ要素を引用します。
Webサイトで確認できない推測は入れません。
次の文で自社サービスと自然につながるようにします。

sections.title: Webサイトのシグナルから作る
sections.copy: 最初に、最も確認しやすいシグナルを1つ選びます。CTAが下にある、事例が見つけにくい、ブログ更新が止まっている、サービスページが薄いなど、相手もすぐ確認できる点が適しています。
items: CTAやファーストビューはWeb制作やCROの切り口に向いています。
コンテンツ更新やサービスページはSEO提案に向いています。
事例やレビューは信頼構築やコンバージョン改善の切り口になります。

sections.title: テンプレート感を減らす
sections.copy: 構成はテンプレート化しても、内容まで同じにしてはいけません。観察点、影響、軽い提案という型だけを使い、実際のWebサイトから見つけた情報を毎回差し替えます。
items: 会社名、ページ位置、観察点は実際の情報に置き換えます。
確認できない主張は削除します。
初回メールでは長い提案ではなく、返信しやすい小さな次の一歩を置きます。

example.title: 営業メール冒頭文の例
example.copy: 御社のトップページは製品の説明が分かりやすい一方で、デモ予約のCTAは少し下まで進まないと見つかりませんでした。
items: 次の文：製品を理解した訪問者が、予約までに一手間増える可能性があります。
軽いCTA：必要であれば、ファーストビューで試せる改善案を3つだけお送りします。
避けたい書き方：御社のサイトが素晴らしいと思い、ご連絡しました。

faqs.question: 冒頭文は必ず個別化すべきですか？
faqs.answer: 構成は共通で構いませんが、観察点は個別化すべきです。同じ内容を使い回すと一斉送信に見えます。

faqs.question: 冒頭文はどのくらいの長さがよいですか？
faqs.answer: 1〜2文で十分です。スマートフォンでも読めて、相手のWebサイトを見たことが伝わる長さにします。

faqs.question: AIで自動生成してもよいですか？
faqs.answer: よいですが、送信前に必ずソースメモを確認してください。確認できない主張が入っていないかを見ることが重要です。

ja.title: 個人プロフェッショナルの営業リード精査：保存する前に適合度を見る
ja.seoTitle: 営業リード 精査ワークフロー | LeadCue

heroBullets: Qualification should happen before a prospect enters personal outreach.

薄いページ データは信号を弱める可能性があるため、信頼性が重要です。

sections.title: 適合性と証拠の品質を分離する
sections.copy: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer. Confidence answers whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust the recommendation. Both are needed before you save the prospect.
items: 適合には、業界、企業タイプ、サービスのニーズ、およびオファーの関連性が含まれます。
信頼性には、ページの深さ、ソースの明瞭さ、信号の特異性が含まれます。
A high-fit prospect with weak evidence may need manual review before outreach.

sections.title: 保存しきい値を定義する
sections.copy: Set a clear threshold for when a prospect is worth saving. Without one, it is easy to keep too many companies that have no usable outreach angle.
items: フィット スコアが高く、少なくとも 2 つの Webサイト キューがその角度をサポートしている場合に保存します。
適合性は強いが信頼性が中程度の場合にレビューします。
利用可能なメモが一般的なもの、またはオファーに関係のないもののみの場合はスキップしてください。

sections.title: Qualification matrix
sections.copy: A clear matrix keeps high-fit prospects with weak evidence out of outreach while preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: High fit and high confidence: save as a Prospect Card and prioritize outreach.
High fit and low confidence: review another page before export.
Low fit and strong signal: archive the note unless your offer or ICP changes.

sections.title: 輸出に資格を持ち込む
sections.copy: The qualification context should not disappear when a prospect leaves the research tool. Export fields should include fit score, signal summary, first line, source notes, follow-up status, and saved/exported status.
items: Use status fields to prevent duplicate work.
Keep source notes in exports or your own notes for review.
Export only saved prospects when building outreach lists.

example.title: 実用的な保存ルール
example.copy: Save a prospect when it matches the ICP, has at least two source-backed website cues, and has a first line you would be comfortable sending.
items: 適合スコア: 80 以上。
信頼度: 70% 以上。
Status: saved, reviewed, and ready for CSV export.

faqs.question: 適合スコアのしきい値とは何ですか?
faqs.answer: 優先度の高いアウトリーチの場合は 80 から始めて、スキャン量だけではなく返信の質と予約された会議に基づいて調整します。

faqs.question: Should weak-fit prospects be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not always. Keep them out of immediate outreach, but use notes to understand whether the ICP or scoring rules need refinement.

faqs.question: 個人プロフェッショナルは資格の品質をどのようにレビューすべきですか?
faqs.answer: Review saved prospects weekly by fit score, confidence, reply quality, and whether the first line matched a real source note.

faqs.question: Web制作会社はどんな見込み客を優先すべきですか？
faqs.answer: サイト上に明確な改善余地があり、事業規模や単価から投資余地がありそうな企業を優先します。

faqs.question: 相手のサイトの悪い点を直接言ってよいですか？
faqs.answer: 強い否定は避け、確認できる観察と改善機会として伝えるほうが返信されやすくなります。

見込み客リサーチのワークフロー
heroBullets: Webサイトをざっと調べて、ビジネス関連の具体的な観察を 1 つ見つけます。
観察結果を購入者の可能性のある結果に結び付けます。
返信を簡単にするための小さな次のステップを提案します。

sections.title: ステップ 1: 適切なページを選択する
sections.copy: ホームページ、価格設定ページ、製品ページ、サービス ページ、またはメイン ナビゲーションから始めます。これらのページでは通常、完全な監査を必要とせずに最も強力なビジネス シグナルが明らかになります。
items: 見出し、CTA、証拠、ナビゲーション、コンバージョン パスを確認します。
些細なデザインの好みではなく、購入者の摩擦を探してください。
後でメモを確認できるようにソースをキャプチャします。

sections.title: ステップ 2: 観察結果を翻訳する
sections.copy: Webサイトの観察は、ビジネス効果の可能性を説明する場合にアウトリーチの角度になります。目標はすべてを診断することではありません。会話を始めるための信頼できる理由を作るためです。
items: 観察: デモ CTA は最初のスクロールの下にあります。
考えられる影響: 関心の高い訪問者は、注目が最も高い間は行動を起こさない可能性があります。
オファー: ヒーロー セクションのアイデアを 3 つ送信してください。

sections.title: ステップ 3: 電子メールを作成する
sections.copy: 電子メールはコンパクトにしてください。冒頭文は関連性を証明するために使用し、2 番目の文は機会を説明するために使用し、CTA はアイデアを送信する許可を求めるために使用します。
items: 光スキャンによる確実性を誇張しないでください。
すべてのサービスをファーストタッチで投げないでください。
返信は簡単にしてください:「送ってもいいですか?」

example.title: コールドメールの角度の例
example.copy: 製品ストーリーは明確ですが、金融購入者の証明とデモ CTA が最初のスクロールの後に表示されることに気付きました。そのため、すでに製品を理解している訪問者によるデモの予約にコストがかかる可能性があります。
items: 冒頭文: 具体的かつ検証可能。
機会: 予約されたデモに関連付けられます。
CTA: 3 つのクイックフィックスを送信します。

faqs.question: 1 つのメールで言及する Webサイトのシグナルは何個必要ですか?
faqs.answer: 通常、1 つの強い信号で十分です。シグナルについて言及しすぎると、電子メールが監査ダンプのように感じられる可能性があります。

faqs.question: CTA は会議を要請する必要がありますか?
faqs.answer: コールド ファースト タッチの場合は、軽い CTA の方が効果的であることがよくあります。会議を依頼する前に、アイデア、分解、または例を送ることを申し出てください。

faqs.question: Webサイトに明らかな問題がない場合はどうなりますか?
faqs.answer: Do not force it. Either scan another page, save the prospect for later review, or skip it until you have a stronger reason to reach out.

ja.title: 見込み客の Webサイトをアウトリーチに追加する前にスコアリングする方法
ja.seoTitle: 見込み客の Webサイトをアウトリーチのためにスコアリングする方法 | LeadCueガイド
ja.description: この実用的なスコアリング フレームワークを使用して、ICP 適合性、Webサイトの手がかり、信頼性、連絡経路、アウトリーチの準備状況によって見込み Webサイトを評価します。
ja.intent: Give solo operators a scoring method they can use before saving and exporting prospects.
secondaryKeywords: 見込み顧客適合スコア
Webサイトの販売シグナル
heroBullets: Fit score tells you whether the prospect matches your offer.
信号品質により、アウトリーチの理由が強いかどうかがわかります。
Export readiness tells you whether the prospect is ready for the next step.

sections.title: ICP 適合を最初にスコア付けします
sections.copy: Start with the company itself. If it does not match your offer focus, even a clear website gap may not be worth pursuing.
items: 業界と市場への適合。
会社の規模または複雑さの適合。
Web デザイン、SEO、マーケティング、または成長サービスとの関連性を提供します。

sections.title: Webサイトのキューをスコアリングする
sections.copy: Webサイトの合図は、冒頭文をサポートするのに十分具体的であり、ビジネスチャンスを示唆するのに十分な意味のあるものである必要があります。弱い手がかりは、一般的、主観的、またはオファーとは無関係です。
items: 強力な手がかり: デモ CTA はスクロールせずに見える範囲に表示されません。
中程度の手がかり: ブログは非アクティブのようですが、ソースの日付は不明です。
弱い手がかり: Webサイトはもっとモダンに見えるかもしれない。

sections.title: Weighted scoring matrix
sections.copy: A weighted model gives you the same decision language each time and makes it easier to tune the workflow after outreach runs.
items: ICP fit: 40 points for industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
Opportunity signal: 35 points for source specificity, business implication, and offer relevance.
Readiness: 25 points for confidence, contact path, first line, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: スコアのエクスポートの準備状況
sections.copy: A prospect is not ready just because it has a high score. The handoff fields need to be complete enough for you to use without redoing the research.
items: 冒頭文はコピー可能です。
ソースノートが添付されています。
Follow-up status and export fields are set.

example.title: 単純なスコアリング モデル
example.copy: ICP 適合には 40 ポイント、Webサイトの合図には 35 ポイント、信頼度には 15 ポイント、連絡経路と輸出準備には 10 ポイントを使用します。
items: 80-100: 保存して優先順位を付けます。
65-79: アウトリーチの前に手動で確認します。
65 未満: スキップするか、後で再度アクセスしてください。

faqs.question: スコアリングは完全に自動化されるべきですか?
faqs.answer: Automation should create a recommendation, but you should review edge cases and use reply quality to tune the model.

faqs.question: 適合性と自信の違いは何ですか?
faqs.answer: Fit measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures how strong and complete the available evidence is.

faqs.question: スコアの低い見込み客はエクスポートする必要がありますか?
faqs.answer: Usually no. Exporting weak prospects creates downstream work and lowers outreach quality.

ja.category: Webサイト監査のアウトリーチ
ja.title: Webサイト診断を使った営業メール
ja.seoTitle: Webサイト診断 営業メール | LeadCue
ja.description: Webサイト診断の具体的な気づきを先に伝え、売り込みではなく役立つ提案として会話を始めます。
ja.intent: 軽いWebサイト診断を、返信されやすい初回営業メールに変える方法を説明します。
ja.primaryKeyword: Webサイト診断 営業メール
secondaryKeywords: サイト改善 メール
営業前 Webサイト チェック
heroBullets: 売り込みの前に、相手が確認できるWebサイトの気づきを送ります。
診断は軽く絞り、返信しやすい提案にします。
Web制作、SEO、CRO、グロース支援の初回アプローチに向いています。

sections.title: Webサイト診断型アプローチの考え方
sections.copy: Webサイト診断型アプローチは、無料で完全な監査をすることではありません。相手のサイトで目立つシグナルを1つか2つだけ選び、役立つ会話のきっかけにする方法です。
items: 最も強い観察点だけを選び、問題を並べすぎないようにします。
観察点を転換率、信頼、検索、問い合わせ導線に結び付けます。
最初の依頼は商談ではなく、短い改善案の送付にします。

sections.title: 初回メールに向く診断ポイント
sections.copy: 初回メールでは、相手がすぐ確認できる軽い診断点が向いています。CTAが見つけにくい、事例導線が弱い、サービスページが薄い、コンテンツ更新が止まっているといった点です。
items: ファーストビューとCTAはWeb制作やCROの提案に向いています。
事例や実績は信頼構築やランディングページ改善の切り口になります。
コンテンツやサービスページはSEOや需要獲得の切り口になります。

sections.title: 低い負担で返信できるCTAにする
sections.copy: 最初から30分の打ち合わせを求めるより、3つの改善案を送る提案のほうが返信しやすくなります。相手が小さく受け取れる次の一歩にしましょう。
items: 「少しお話しできますか」より「3つだけ案を送ります」が軽く見えます。
初回メールに長い監査レポートを添付しないようにします。
ソースメモを残し、フォローアップでも同じ根拠を参照できるようにします。

example.title: Webサイト診断メールの例
example.copy: トップページでは製品価値が伝わりますが、顧客事例への導線が主ナビゲーションから少し見つけにくく、検討中の買い手が証拠にたどり着くまで時間がかかりそうです。
items: 切り口：信頼材料への導線が弱い。
影響：予約前に必要な安心材料を見つけにくい可能性があります。
次の一歩：事例と予約CTAを見せやすくする案を3つ送る。

faqs.question: 無料コンサルになりすぎませんか？
faqs.answer: 範囲を絞れば問題ありません。初回メールでは1つの観察点と小さな提案だけで十分です。

faqs.question: どの企業にも使えますか？
faqs.answer: いいえ。Webサイト上に確認できる改善点があり、自社サービスと強く関連する場合にだけ使うべきです。

faqs.question: スクリーンショットを添付すべきですか？
faqs.answer: 初回メールでは短さを優先し、返信後に送るほうが自然な場合が多いです。

ko.title: 잠재고객 리서치 툴: 웹사이트 신호로 리드를 선별
ko.description: 웹사이트의 CTA, 고객 증거, 콘텐츠, 연락 경로를 검토해 아웃리치할 가치가 있는 B2B 리드를 찾습니다.
ko.intent: 웹사이트 신호를 기준으로 연락할 가치가 있는 잠재고객을 선별하는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 일반 연락처 리스트가 아니라 잠재고객 웹사이트에서 판단을 시작합니다.
첫 화면, 고객 증거, CTA, 콘텐츠 업데이트, 연락 경로로 영업할 이유를 찾습니다.
각 관찰을 콜드메일 첫 문장, 제안 각도, CSV 메모로 바꿉니다.

sections.title: 웹사이트 기반 리드 발굴의 의미
sections.copy: 웹사이트 기반 리드 발굴은 단순히 연락처를 더 모으는 일이 아닙니다. 연락하기 전에 잠재고객의 공개 웹사이트를 보고, 우리 서비스와 맞는지, 자연스럽게 연락할 만한 이유가 있는지 판단하는 과정입니다.
items: 웹디자인, SEO, CRO, 그로스 마케팅, 메시지 개선 서비스를 제공하는 개인 사용자에 적합합니다.
목표는 더 많은 회사를 찾는 것이 아니라 연락할 가치가 있는 회사를 고르는 것입니다.
좋은 결과물은 왜 지금 연락해야 하는지를 설명할 수 있어야 합니다.

sections.title: 확인해야 할 웹사이트 신호
sections.copy: 좋은 신호는 단순한 감상이 아니라 비즈니스 결과와 연결됩니다. CTA가 늦게 보이거나, 사례가 찾기 어렵거나, 서비스 페이지가 얕거나, 콘텐츠가 오래 멈춰 있다면 아웃리치의 자연스러운 출발점이 될 수 있습니다.
items: 첫 화면: 헤드라인, 가치 제안, CTA, 신뢰 요소가 명확한지 봅니다.
신뢰 증거: 사례, 후기, 로고, 성과 데이터가 쉽게 보이는지 확인합니다.
콘텐츠와 경로: 업데이트, 서비스 페이지 깊이, 연락 경로가 구매자 행동을 돕는지 봅니다.

sections.title: 아웃리치 문맥으로 바꾸기
sections.copy: 콜드메일에서 '웹사이트를 봤다'는 말만으로는 부족합니다. 무엇을 봤는지, 그것이 어떤 영향을 줄 수 있는지, 어떤 작은 제안을 할 수 있는지를 짧게 연결해야 실제 리서치처럼 느껴집니다.
items: 첫 문장에는 하나의 구체적인 웹사이트 관찰만 담습니다.
다음 문장에서 전환, 신뢰, SEO, 문의 경로와의 관계를 설명합니다.
첫 요청은 가볍게 두고, 예를 들어 개선 아이디어 3개를 보내겠다고 제안합니다.

example.title: 웹사이트 기반 리드 메모 예시
example.copy: Northstar Analytics는 제품 설명은 명확하지만, 데모 CTA와 재무개인 사용자 대상 고객 증거가 첫 화면에는 보이지 않습니다.
items: 신호: 데모 예약 경로가 아래쪽에 있어 관심이 높은 방문자가 한 단계를 더 거쳐야 합니다.
영업 각도: 제품을 이해한 방문자가 바로 행동할 이유를 놓칠 수 있습니다.
제안: 첫 화면 CTA, 증거 블록, 사례 경로에 대한 빠른 아이디어 3개를 보냅니다.

faqs.question: 웹사이트 기반 리드 발굴은 스크래핑인가요?
faqs.answer: 아닙니다. 공개된 웹사이트 정보를 검토해 리드 품질과 연락 이유를 판단하는 과정입니다. 연락처 DB 구매나 소셜 스크래핑과 다릅니다.

faqs.question: 어떤 개인 사용자에 가장 잘 맞나요?
faqs.answer: 웹디자인, SEO, CRO, 그로스 마케팅, 메시지 개선처럼 웹사이트에서 보이는 문제와 서비스를 직접 연결할 수 있는 개인 사용자에 잘 맞습니다.

faqs.question: CSV에는 무엇을 남겨야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 회사명, URL, 적합도 점수, 주요 웹사이트 신호, 소스 메모, 콜드메일 첫 문장, 담당자, 단계, 내보내기 상태를 남기는 것이 좋습니다.

ko.title: AI website prospecting tool: qualify prospects before outreach
ko.seoTitle: AI Website Prospecting Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
ko.description: Use AI website prospecting to find opportunity signals, score prospect fit, and prepare source-backed first lines before outreach.
ko.intent: Define the LeadCue method for solo professionals who research prospect websites before outreach.
heroBullets: Start with what the prospect's website already proves instead of a generic contact record.
Use opportunity signals and fit scoring to decide whether the prospect deserves outreach.
Turn the research into a Prospect Card that carries context into CSV or email workflows.

sections.title: What AI website prospecting changes
sections.copy: Traditional outbound often starts with a list and searches for a contact. AI website prospecting starts with the company website and asks whether there is a credible reason to contact the prospect at all.
items: The first decision is qualification, not email discovery.
The research output is a source-backed reason to contact, not a vague personalization line.
The handoff keeps website evidence, fit score, first line, and export fields together.

sections.title: Workflow matrix
sections.copy: The workflow should move from public evidence to prospect judgment to outreach preparation without losing context between tools.
items: Review: inspect homepage, proof, CTA, service pages, content freshness, and contact path.
Qualify: score ICP fit, signal strength, confidence, and outreach readiness.
Prepare: save a Prospect Card with source notes, first line, outreach angle, follow-up status, and export status.

sections.title: When it is the right motion
sections.copy: Website prospecting is strongest when your offer can be connected to visible website improvements or missed opportunities.
items: Web design freelancers can use CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion signals.
SEO consultants can use thin pages, stale content, weak internal paths, and missing intent pages.
Growth consultants can use positioning, campaign path, lead magnet, and proof gaps.

example.title: Website-first research example
example.copy: Instead of saving a SaaS company because it matches an industry filter, you save it because the site has a clear product story, hidden finance proof, and no above-the-fold demo CTA.
items: Signal: proof and CTA are not visible when intent is highest.
Fit: your offer is conversion-focused website improvements.
Output: a Prospect Card with a verifiable first line and export-ready note.

faqs.question: Is AI website prospecting the same as scraping?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow uses visible public website evidence, human review, and source-backed notes. It is not designed to scrape social platforms or build hidden contact intelligence.

faqs.question: Who should use AI website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for SEO consultants, web design freelancers, CRO specialists, growth consultants, positioning consultants, and solo founders because their offers can be tied to website evidence.

faqs.question: What should be produced before outreach starts?
faqs.answer: A saved Prospect Card should include fit score, opportunity signals, source notes, first line, reason to contact, and export status.

ko.title: AI prospect research tool: the step before cold email
ko.seoTitle: AI Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
ko.description: Learn why solo professionals need a prospect research tool that qualifies companies, preserves source evidence, and prepares outreach context before sending cold email.
ko.intent: Position LeadCue as the research layer between prospect lists and outreach execution.
heroBullets: A spreadsheet stores prospects after they are accepted; a research layer decides whether they should be accepted.
An email tool sends messages; a research layer prepares the source-backed reason to send one.
A website research layer keeps Prospect Cards, fit scores, and opportunity signals together.

sections.title: Where the research layer sits
sections.copy: Many solo professionals have contact sources, spreadsheets, and email tools, but no dedicated place to decide whether a website-backed prospect is worth outreach.
items: Before export: qualify the website and avoid adding weak prospects.
Before email: prepare first lines and context that can be verified.
Before follow-up: preserve why the prospect was selected, skipped, or reviewed.

sections.title: Decision table for tool boundaries
sections.copy: A focused research layer should not pretend to replace every outbound tool. Its value is to improve the quality of prospects and context handed downstream.
items: Contact database: finds people or emails; research layer explains why the company is worth contacting.
Pipeline tool: manages follow-up status; research layer creates the source-backed context before export.
Email sequencer: sends campaigns; research layer prepares claims, first lines, and notes before sending.

sections.title: Signals the layer should preserve
sections.copy: The layer becomes useful when every saved prospect carries enough context for you to understand the decision later without repeating the research.
items: Website evidence: page observation, source path, and buyer implication.
Qualification: ICP fit, confidence, save threshold, and opportunity category.
Handoff: first line, short email context, export fields, status, and follow-up notes.

example.title: Research layer handoff example
example.copy: You review a website, save a Prospect Card, and export only the qualified prospect with fit score, source notes, first line, and a short outreach angle attached.
items: The exported row receives context, not just a URL.
The email writer can verify the claim before sending.
You can review why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Does a website research layer replace an outreach tool?
faqs.answer: No. It sits before your outreach workflow and prepares qualified, source-backed prospect context for export or handoff.

faqs.question: Does it replace an email finder?
faqs.answer: No. Email finders help locate contacts. A website research layer helps explain whether the prospect is worth contacting and what the outreach should reference.

faqs.question: What makes the layer useful for solo professionals?
faqs.answer: Solo professionals can connect their offer to visible website opportunities, then save that evidence as a reusable Prospect Card.

ko.title: Website opportunity signals solo professionals can use before outreach
ko.seoTitle: Website Opportunity Signals for Solo Outreach | LeadCue
ko.description: Use website opportunity signals to spot CTA, proof, SEO, content, positioning, and timing cues that can support source-backed personal outreach.
ko.intent: Give solo professionals a signal taxonomy for turning website observations into outreach reasons.
heroBullets: Opportunity signals connect a visible page observation to a business-relevant outreach angle.
The best signals are specific enough for a buyer to verify and relevant enough for your offer.
Weak signals should trigger review or skip decisions instead of automatic export.

sections.title: Signal taxonomy
sections.copy: A useful taxonomy keeps you from treating subjective design opinions as sales evidence. Each signal should map to a buyer outcome and your offer.
items: Conversion signals: hidden CTA, unclear next step, confusing navigation, weak contact path.
Trust signals: missing case studies, buried proof, no results, weak testimonials, unclear customer segments.
Content signals: stale blog, thin service pages, missing use cases, weak internal linking, outdated resources.

sections.title: How to score signal strength
sections.copy: Signal strength depends on visibility, relevance, and confidence. A visible issue that matches your offer is stronger than a broad guess about the business.
items: Strong: the source is visible, the implication is clear, and the offer can help.
Medium: the pattern is plausible but needs another source or manual review.
Weak: the note is subjective, generic, or disconnected from your offer.

sections.title: Turn signals into outreach context
sections.copy: Signals become useful when they are converted into a reason to contact, a first line, and a Prospect Card note you can verify later.
items: Observation: what the page shows and where it appears.
Implication: why it may affect conversion, trust, discovery, or buyer confidence.
Next step: a low-pressure suggestion such as sending three ideas or a short teardown.

example.title: Opportunity signal example
example.copy: The product page is clear, but the strongest customer proof appears below the main CTA and the navigation does not surface industry-specific examples.
items: Category: trust proof and conversion path.
Reason to contact: buyers may need proof earlier before booking.
First line: mention the proof placement and offer a small set of ideas.

faqs.question: How many opportunity signals should a prospect need?
faqs.answer: One strong signal can be enough for a first touch. Two or three source-backed signals make qualification more reliable.

faqs.question: Are opportunity signals the same as a full audit?
faqs.answer: No. They are lightweight sales research cues used before outreach. A full audit can happen after the prospect engages.

faqs.question: What should happen when the signal is weak?
faqs.answer: Do not export the prospect automatically. Mark it for review, scan another page, or skip until a stronger reason to contact appears.

ko.title: How to find a reason to contact a prospect from their website
ko.seoTitle: Find a Reason to Contact Prospects from Website Evidence | LeadCue
ko.description: Find a credible reason to contact a prospect by reviewing website signals, qualifying fit, and turning source evidence into a useful first line.
ko.intent: Help solo operators solve the core problem of why this prospect should be contacted now.
heroBullets: A good reason to contact is specific, visible, relevant to the offer, and easy to verify.
The website should provide evidence, not just a name to personalize around.
If there is no credible reason, the prospect should be skipped or reviewed instead of exported.

sections.title: A simple qualification order
sections.copy: Start with prospect fit, then inspect the website for evidence, then decide whether the observation supports a useful first touch.
items: Fit: does the company match your ICP, offer, and target market?
Evidence: does the website show a specific opportunity signal or gap?
Message: can the observation become a first line and next step without overclaiming?

sections.title: Reason-to-contact checklist
sections.copy: Before saving the prospect, you should be able to answer five questions that make the outreach defensible.
items: What did we see on the website, and where did we see it?
Why might it matter to the buyer?
Why is your offer relevant to that observation?

sections.title: Bad reasons to avoid
sections.copy: Weak reasons make outreach feel automated even when a first line is technically personalized. The problem is not word choice; it is lack of relevance.
items: Vague compliments such as liking the website without a specific observation.
Unverified claims about performance, rankings, revenue, or conversion impact.
Signals that do not connect to your offer or buyer outcome.

example.title: Reason to contact example
example.copy: The website targets finance buyers, but the homepage does not surface finance proof or a clear demo CTA above the first scroll.
items: Observation: proof and CTA placement are visible website details.
Implication: high-intent visitors may not see enough trust before acting.
Next step: offer three hero and proof-section ideas.

faqs.question: What if a website has no obvious issue?
faqs.answer: Do not force a reason. Scan another important page, review a different offer angle, or skip the prospect until stronger evidence appears.

faqs.question: Should the first email include every reason?
faqs.answer: No. Usually one strong reason is enough. Too many observations can make a first touch feel like an unsolicited audit.

faqs.question: Can a reason to contact be positive?
faqs.answer: Yes, if it is specific and relevant. The reason can be an opportunity to build on a strength, not only a problem to fix.

ko.title: Source-backed prospect notes for safer personal outreach
ko.seoTitle: Source-Backed Prospect Notes for Personal Outreach | LeadCue
ko.description: Create source-backed prospect notes that keep website evidence, uncertainty, first lines, and export context attached to each saved prospect.
ko.intent: Show solo professionals how to preserve evidence so outreach copy can be reviewed before sending.
heroBullets: Source-backed notes help you verify claims before they appear in outreach.
They reduce generic personalization by tying each first line to a visible page observation.
They make export safer because context travels with the prospect.

sections.title: What source-backed means
sections.copy: A source-backed note states what was observed, where it was observed, why it may matter, and how confident you should be before using it in outreach.
items: Source: page type, URL path, section, or visible website area.
Observation: the exact cue, not a broad interpretation.
Confidence: whether the evidence is strong, incomplete, or needs manual review.

sections.title: Note structure
sections.copy: The best notes are compact enough for export but complete enough that you can understand and verify the outreach angle later.
items: Observed cue: demo CTA appears below the first scroll.
Possible implication: visitors may need a clearer next step when intent is highest.
Outbound use: ask permission to send three hero CTA and proof-section ideas.

sections.title: Review before sending
sections.copy: Source-backed notes should improve judgment, not remove it. Review the source and remove any claim that cannot be supported.
items: Avoid revenue, ranking, or conversion claims without evidence.
Keep uncertainty visible when a signal is incomplete.
Use the note as context, not as a final message that must be sent unchanged.

example.title: Source-backed note example
example.copy: Homepage review: the product story is clear, but the finance proof and demo CTA appear after the first scroll. Possible angle: make buyer trust and next step visible earlier.
items: Specific source: homepage first viewport.
Clear implication: proof and next step visibility.
Safe use: offer ideas without claiming confirmed conversion loss.

faqs.question: Should source notes be exported?
faqs.answer: Yes. Put short source notes in export fields or notes so you can verify why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Can AI-generated notes be used directly?
faqs.answer: They should be reviewed first. The note must match visible evidence before it becomes outreach copy.

faqs.question: How long should a source-backed note be?
faqs.answer: Long enough to preserve the observation, implication, and source, but short enough to scan during outreach review.

ko.title: ICP fit score for solo website prospecting
ko.seoTitle: ICP Fit Score for Solo Website Prospecting | LeadCue
ko.description: Build an ICP fit score that combines prospect fit, website opportunity signals, evidence confidence, and export readiness before personal outreach.
ko.intent: Help solo professionals separate prospect fit from signal confidence and outreach readiness.
heroBullets: Fit score answers whether the company matches your offer.
Confidence score answers whether the website evidence is strong enough to use.
Export readiness answers whether the prospect can move downstream without rework.

sections.title: Fit score model
sections.copy: A practical score should combine the prospect profile with website evidence and handoff readiness instead of treating all signals as equal.
items: 40 points: ICP match across industry, region, company type, size, and service fit.
35 points: opportunity signal quality, source specificity, and buyer implication.
25 points: confidence, contact path, first-line readiness, follow-up status, and export fields.

sections.title: Decision thresholds
sections.copy: Thresholds help you avoid exporting weak prospects while still preserving companies that deserve manual review.
items: 80-100: save and prioritize for outreach after source review.
65-79: review manually or scan another page before export.
Below 65: skip, archive, or revisit when the ICP or offer changes.

sections.title: Common scoring mistakes
sections.copy: A high fit score is only useful when the evidence is strong enough to support a real outreach reason.
items: Giving high scores to prospects that match the industry but have no visible reason to contact.
Mixing prospect fit with confidence so weak evidence looks stronger than it is.
Exporting prospects before first line, source notes, and export fields are complete.

example.title: Fit score example
example.copy: A B2B SaaS company matches your ICP, shows a hidden demo CTA and missing proof path, and has enough source evidence for a first line.
items: ICP fit: 36/40.
Signal quality: 28/35.
Readiness: 21/25. Total: 85, save after review.

faqs.question: Is fit score the same as confidence score?
faqs.answer: No. Fit score measures prospect relevance. Confidence measures whether the available website evidence is strong enough to trust.

faqs.question: What score should qualify a lead?
faqs.answer: Start with 80 for high-priority outreach, then adjust based on reply quality, booked meetings, and manual QA results.

faqs.question: Should low-score leads be deleted?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Keep them out of outreach, but use their notes to improve ICP rules or review later.

ko.title: 개인 전문가용 잠재고객 리서치 툴 선택 기준
ko.seoTitle: 개인 전문가 잠재고객 리서치 툴 | LeadCue
ko.description: 개인 전문가가 웹사이트 근거, 적합도 점수, 아웃리치 문맥, CSV 내보내기를 기준으로 잠재고객 리서치 툴을 고르는 방법입니다.
ko.intent: 영업 전 리서치를 반복 가능한 워크플로우로 만들고 싶은 개인 전문가를 위한 제품형 가이드입니다.
heroBullets: 리드 수집보다 먼저 연락할 이유와 근거를 정리합니다.
웹사이트 관찰을 적합도 점수와 첫 문장으로 연결합니다.
저장된 카드가 CSV와 CSV으로 그대로 넘어가도록 설계합니다.

sections.title: 개인 전문가 리서치 툴이 해결해야 할 일
sections.copy: 좋은 리서치 툴은 회사명 목록을 더 많이 주는 데서 끝나지 않습니다. 어떤 계정이 저장할 가치가 있는지, 사이트에서 어떤 근거를 봤는지, 첫 연락에서 어떤 각도로 말해야 하는지를 함께 정리해야 합니다.
items: ICP 적합도와 웹사이트 증거를 한 화면에서 확인
서비스별 영업 각도와 첫 문장을 함께 저장
CSV으로 넘겨도 근거가 사라지지 않는 필드 구성

sections.title: 선택할 때 봐야 할 기준
sections.copy: 개인 전문가 개인 사용자은 반복 작업이 많기 때문에 점수, 검토 큐, 내보내기 흐름이 중요합니다. 한 명이 빠르게 검토해도 다음 담당자가 왜 이 계정을 저장했는지 바로 이해할 수 있어야 합니다.
items: 적합도 점수와 신뢰도 점수가 분리되어 있는가
웹사이트 신호가 페이지 근거와 함께 저장되는가
HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive 또는 CSV로 쉽게 넘길 수 있는가

example.title: 선택 기준 예시
example.copy: 웹디자인 개인 전문가라면 CTA, 사례, 문의 경로 같은 화면 신호를 스캔하고, 저장 기준을 넘는 계정만 첫 문장과 함께 내보내는 툴이 실무에 맞습니다.
items: 주요 신호: CTA와 사례 노출
저장 기준: 적합도 80점 이상
내보내기: CSV 메모에 근거와 첫 문장 포함

faqs.question: 리드 데이터베이스와 무엇이 다른가요?
faqs.answer: 리드 데이터베이스는 연락처를 찾는 데 강합니다. LeadCue 같은 리서치 툴은 연락하기 전에 왜 이 계정이 좋은지와 무엇을 말할지를 정리하는 데 초점이 있습니다.

faqs.question: 소규모 개인 전문가도 필요한가요?
faqs.answer: 매주 일정량의 아웃바운드를 반복한다면 필요합니다. 특히 저장 기준과 첫 문장 품질을 일정하게 유지해야 할 때 효과가 큽니다.

ko.title: 웹사이트 근거로 쓰는 콜드메일 첫 문장
ko.description: 잠재고객 웹사이트에서 확인한 구체적인 신호로 자연스럽고 검증 가능한 콜드메일 첫 문장을 만듭니다.
ko.intent: 잠재고객 웹사이트 근거로 자연스럽고 검증 가능한 콜드메일 첫 문장을 만드는 방법을 보여줍니다.
heroBullets: 콜드메일 첫 문장은 잠재고객 웹사이트에서 확인한 사실에서 시작합니다.
짧고 구체적이며, 자연스럽게 제안으로 이어져야 합니다.
발송 전 검토할 수 있도록 소스 메모를 함께 남깁니다.

sections.title: 좋은 첫 문장이 작동하는 이유
sections.copy: 좋은 첫 문장은 상대에게 실제로 웹사이트를 확인했다는 느낌을 줍니다. 하지만 과하게 친한 척할 필요는 없습니다. 확인 가능한 사실을 짧게 쓰고, 그것이 어떤 비즈니스 결과와 연결되는지 자연스럽게 보여주면 됩니다.
items: 막연한 칭찬 대신 특정 페이지 요소를 언급합니다.
웹사이트에서 확인할 수 없는 추측은 넣지 않습니다.
다음 문장에서 우리 서비스와 자연스럽게 연결합니다.

sections.title: 웹사이트 신호에서 첫 문장 만들기
sections.copy: 가장 확인하기 쉬운 신호 하나를 먼저 고릅니다. CTA가 아래에 있거나, 사례가 찾기 어렵거나, 블로그가 오래 멈췄거나, 서비스 페이지가 얕은 것처럼 상대도 바로 확인할 수 있는 내용이 좋습니다.
items: CTA와 첫 화면 신호는 웹디자인이나 전환 개선 제안에 적합합니다.
콘텐츠 업데이트와 서비스 페이지 깊이는 SEO 제안에 적합합니다.
사례와 후기 신호는 신뢰 구축과 랜딩페이지 개선 제안에 적합합니다.

sections.title: 템플릿 느낌 줄이기
sections.copy: 구조는 템플릿화할 수 있지만 내용까지 같으면 안 됩니다. 관찰, 영향, 작은 제안이라는 틀은 유지하되 실제 웹사이트에서 본 정보를 매번 바꿔야 합니다.
items: 회사명, 페이지 위치, 관찰 내용을 실제 정보로 바꿉니다.
확인할 수 없는 주장은 삭제합니다.
첫 메일에는 긴 제안서가 아니라 답장하기 쉬운 작은 다음 단계를 둡니다.

example.title: 콜드메일 첫 문장 예시
example.copy: 홈페이지에서 제품 설명은 명확하게 보였지만, 데모 예약 CTA는 조금 내려가야 확인할 수 있었습니다.
items: 다음 문장: 제품을 이해한 방문자가 예약까지 한 단계를 더 거칠 수 있습니다.
가벼운 CTA: 괜찮다면 첫 화면 CTA와 증거 블록에 대한 아이디어 3개를 보내드리겠습니다.
피해야 할 문장: 웹사이트가 좋아 보여서 연락드렸습니다.

faqs.question: 첫 문장은 꼭 개인화해야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 구조는 같아도 되지만 관찰 내용은 개인화해야 합니다. 같은 내용을 반복하면 대량 발송처럼 보입니다.

faqs.question: 첫 문장은 얼마나 길어야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 1~2문장이면 충분합니다. 모바일에서도 쉽게 읽히고, 상대 웹사이트를 봤다는 사실이 전달되어야 합니다.

faqs.question: AI로 자동 생성해도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 가능합니다. 다만 발송 전 소스 메모를 확인해 검증할 수 없는 주장이 들어가지 않았는지 봐야 합니다.

ko.title: 개인 리드 검증: 저장하기 전에 적합도 확인
ko.description: 웹사이트 신호, 적합도 점수, 신뢰도로 연락할 가치가 있는 잠재고객만 저장하고 CSV으로 넘기는 방법입니다.
ko.intent: 개인 전문가가 리스트 품질을 높이고 아웃리치 전에 계정을 선별하는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 캠페인에 넣기 전에 저장할 가치가 있는 계정인지 판단합니다.
적합도와 증거 품질을 분리해 과한 저장을 줄입니다.
담당자가 바로 이해할 수 있는 근거와 다음 행동을 남깁니다.

sections.title: 적합도와 증거 품질을 나눠 봅니다
sections.copy: 적합도는 회사가 우리 제안과 맞는지 판단합니다. 신뢰도는 웹사이트에서 본 근거가 충분히 구체적인지 판단합니다. 둘을 나눠야 좋은 산업군이지만 근거가 약한 계정을 무리하게 저장하지 않습니다.
items: 업종, 서비스 수요, 지역, 회사 규모로 적합도 판단
페이지 깊이와 신호 구체성으로 신뢰도 판단
근거가 약한 계정은 검토 큐에 남기고 바로 내보내지 않기

sections.title: 저장 기준을 개인 사용자 기준으로 만듭니다
sections.copy: 영업 담당자마다 저장 기준이 다르면 CSV에 품질이 다른 계정이 섞입니다. 적합도 점수, 최소 근거 수, 첫 문장 준비 여부를 기준으로 저장 규칙을 정하면 반복 가능한 리드 검증 흐름이 됩니다.
items: 적합도 80점 이상과 근거 2개 이상일 때 저장
첫 문장이 일반적이면 저장 전 보완
내보낸 계정에는 소유자와 단계까지 함께 기록

example.title: 리드 검증 기준 예시
example.copy: 사이트에 리뉴얼 신호가 있고 적합도는 높지만 근거가 한 개뿐이라면 바로 CSV에 넣기보다 검토 큐에서 추가 확인하는 편이 안전합니다.
items: 적합도: 높음
신뢰도: 중간
처리: 저장 전 추가 근거 확인

faqs.question: 좋은 저장 기준은 몇 점인가요?
faqs.answer: 초기에는 80점을 기준으로 두고, 실제 응답률과 미팅 전환을 보며 조정하는 편이 좋습니다.

faqs.question: 낮은 점수 계정은 삭제해야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 바로 삭제하기보다 왜 낮게 나왔는지 기록하면 ICP나 점수 규칙을 개선하는 데 도움이 됩니다.

ko.title: 웹디자인 개인 리드 발굴: 웹사이트에서 리뉴얼 이유 찾기
ko.description: 웹디자인 개인 전문가가 히어로, CTA, 사례, 문의 경로 같은 웹사이트 신호로 리뉴얼 제안 각도를 찾는 방법입니다.
ko.intent: 웹디자인 개인 전문가가 웹사이트 근거로 더 설득력 있는 영업 계정을 선별하는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 히어로, CTA, 사례 구조에서 바로 확인 가능한 리뉴얼 근거를 찾습니다.
막연한 재제안 대신 실제 화면 관찰을 영업 각도로 바꿉니다.
사이트 근거와 첫 문장을 함께 저장해 다음 단계로 넘깁니다.

sections.title: 웹디자인 제안에 잘 맞는 사이트 신호
sections.copy: 웹디자인 개인 전문가는 사이트에서 바로 보이는 마찰을 근거로 제안할 때 응답을 얻기 쉽습니다. 첫 화면 메시지가 흐리거나 CTA가 늦게 보이거나, 사례와 신뢰 요소가 너무 뒤에 있으면 리뉴얼 각도가 분명해집니다.
items: 첫 화면에서 대상 고객과 다음 행동이 분명한가
문의나 데모 CTA가 충분히 눈에 띄는가
사례와 신뢰 요소가 구매 판단 전에 보이는가

sections.title: 리뉴얼 근거를 영업 문장으로 바꾸는 방법
sections.copy: 첫 메일에서 사이트 전체를 비판할 필요는 없습니다. 한 가지 관찰을 짚고, 그것이 신뢰나 전환에 어떤 영향을 줄 수 있는지 연결한 뒤, 작은 개선 아이디어 몇 가지를 보내겠다고 제안하는 편이 자연스럽습니다.
items: 구체적인 화면 위치를 첫 문장에 넣기
관찰을 전환 또는 신뢰 문제와 연결하기
회의 요청 대신 짧은 아이디어 3개를 제안하기

example.title: 웹디자인 영업 각도 예시
example.copy: 홈페이지가 서비스는 잘 설명하지만 첫 화면에서 상담 CTA가 약해, 이미 관심 있는 방문자도 다음 행동을 찾는 데 한 번 더 멈출 수 있습니다.
items: 신호: 첫 화면 CTA 약함
각도: 문의 경로가 전환 의도를 늦춤
다음 단계: 히어로와 사례 노출 개선 아이디어 3개 제안

faqs.question: 웹디자인 영업은 어떤 계정부터 봐야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 사이트 구조, 메시지, CTA, 사례 노출에 분명한 마찰이 있고 서비스 단가가 맞는 계정부터 우선 보세요.

faqs.question: 첫 메일에서 사이트가 별로라고 직접 말해야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 아니요. 확인 가능한 관찰을 개선 기회로 표현하는 쪽이 훨씬 자연스럽고 반응도 좋습니다.

ko.title: SEO 개인 리드 발굴: 콘텐츠 공백에서 제안 이유 찾기
ko.description: SEO 개인 전문가가 콘텐츠 신선도, 서비스 페이지 깊이, 검색 의도 공백으로 영업 각도를 만드는 방법입니다.
ko.intent: SEO 개인 전문가가 웹사이트 근거로 어떤 잠재고객을 우선 공략할지 판단하는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 블로그 업데이트, 서비스 페이지 깊이, 검색 의도 공백을 먼저 봅니다.
보이는 콘텐츠 부족을 구체적인 아웃리치 각도로 바꿉니다.
후속 제안과 CSV 메모까지 같은 근거를 유지합니다.

sections.title: SEO 아웃리치 전에 확인할 사이트 신호
sections.copy: 좋은 SEO 계정은 산업군만으로 결정되지 않습니다. 블로그가 오래 멈췄거나, 서비스 페이지가 얕거나, 구매 직전 검색 의도를 받는 페이지가 없으면 SEO 제안으로 연결하기 좋은 계정일 가능성이 큽니다.
items: 콘텐츠가 최근에도 꾸준히 갱신되는가
서비스 페이지가 구매자 질문에 충분히 답하는가
비교·도입·문제 해결형 검색 의도 페이지가 있는가

sections.title: 콘텐츠 공백을 영업 이유로 만드는 법
sections.copy: 첫 메일에서 'SEO가 필요합니다'라고 쓰는 건 약합니다. 대신 보이는 공백 하나를 짚고, 그 공백 때문에 검색 단계의 고의도 수요를 놓칠 수 있다고 설명하면 더 믿을 만한 각도가 됩니다.
items: 빠진 주제나 페이지 유형을 구체적으로 언급하기
검색 수요와 데모 전환의 연결고리 설명하기
우선순위가 높은 콘텐츠 아이디어 몇 개 제안하기

example.title: SEO 영업 각도 예시
example.copy: 서비스 페이지는 정리되어 있지만, 비교형 콘텐츠와 구매 기준을 설명하는 페이지가 없어 검색 단계 후반 수요를 놓치고 있을 수 있습니다.
items: 신호: 고의도 콘텐츠 공백
각도: 구매 직전 검색 수요를 받지 못함
다음 단계: 우선 테스트할 콘텐츠 주제 3개 제안

faqs.question: 첫 메일에서 키워드를 직접 언급해도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 가능하지만 많이 넣을 필요는 없습니다. 비즈니스와 연결된 주제 1~2개만 언급하는 편이 더 자연스럽습니다.

faqs.question: 사이트만 보고도 SEO 리드를 저장해도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 초기 선별은 가능합니다. 다만 제안 단계로 가기 전에는 검색량, 경쟁도, 현재 노출 데이터까지 함께 확인하는 편이 좋습니다.

ko.title: 마케팅 개인 리드 발굴: 전환 경로에서 영업 각도 찾기
ko.description: 마케팅 개인 전문가가 CTA, 신뢰 요소, 랜딩 구조, 전환 마찰을 근거로 영업 각도를 만드는 방법입니다.
ko.intent: 마케팅 개인 전문가가 사이트 전환 신호를 바탕으로 영업 우선순위와 메시지를 정리하는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 랜딩 구조, CTA, 증거 노출, 전환 경로를 먼저 살핍니다.
추상적인 성장 제안 대신 보이는 전환 마찰을 짚습니다.
연구 메모와 첫 문장을 CSV 전달 문맥까지 그대로 이어갑니다.

sections.title: 마케팅 서비스와 잘 맞는 사이트 신호
sections.copy: 마케팅 기회는 전환 경로 안에 숨어 있는 경우가 많습니다. 제품 설명은 분명하지만 사례가 늦게 나오거나, 유료 유입이 들어와도 다음 행동이 모호하거나, CTA가 흩어져 있으면 좋은 아웃리치 각도가 됩니다.
items: 첫 화면이 타깃 고객과 결과를 바로 설명하는가
증거와 사례가 CTA 전에 충분히 보이는가
문의 또는 데모 경로가 분명한가

sections.title: 전환 마찰을 작은 테스트 제안으로 바꾸기
sections.copy: 첫 메일에서 큰 성장 계획을 파는 것보다, 전환 마찰 하나를 짚고 작은 실험 아이디어를 제안하는 편이 부담이 적고 답장을 얻기 쉽습니다.
items: 보이는 마찰을 신뢰 있게 설명하기
전환율·예약률·CAC와 연결하기
히어로, 증거, CTA 중심의 작은 테스트 제안하기

example.title: 마케팅 아웃리치 각도 예시
example.copy: 제품 설명은 명확하지만 사례와 수치형 증거가 첫 화면 근처에 없어, 광고나 아웃바운드 유입이 바로 예약까지 가지 못할 수 있습니다.
items: 신호: 증거 노출이 늦음
각도: 전환 전에 신뢰 정보가 부족함
다음 단계: 첫 화면 증거와 CTA 테스트 아이디어 3개 제안

faqs.question: 마케팅 개인 전문가는 어떤 계정을 먼저 봐야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 명확한 오퍼와 유입이 이미 있는데, 사이트 전환 경로와 신뢰 설계가 부족한 계정을 우선 보세요.

faqs.question: 첫 메일에서 예산 이야기를 꺼내야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 보통은 아직 이릅니다. 먼저 사이트 근거로 신뢰를 만들고, 이후 대화에서 채널과 예산을 다루는 편이 좋습니다.

ko.title: 잠재고객 웹사이트를 콜드메일 각도로 바꾸는 방법
ko.description: 웹사이트 관찰을 구체적인 콜드메일 첫 문장과 영업 각도로 바꾸는 실전 가이드입니다.
ko.intent: 웹사이트의 실제 근거를 개인화된 콜드메일 각도로 바꾸는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 보이는 사이트 신호를 첫 문장과 영업 각도로 연결합니다.
문제 지적보다 구체적인 관찰과 영향 설명에 집중합니다.
가벼운 다음 단계 CTA까지 함께 설계합니다.

sections.title: 좋은 콜드메일 각도의 구조
sections.copy: 좋은 각도는 사이트에서 본 사실, 그 사실이 줄 수 있는 영향, 그리고 제안할 수 있는 작은 다음 단계로 구성됩니다. 이 구조가 있어야 메일이 연구 기반으로 읽히고 템플릿처럼 보이지 않습니다.
items: 첫 문장에 검증 가능한 관찰 넣기
관찰을 전환·신뢰·검색과 연결하기
다음 단계는 짧고 부담 없게 제안하기

sections.title: 사이트 신호를 메일 문장으로 바꾸는 방법
sections.copy: CTA가 늦게 보이거나 사례가 숨겨져 있거나 서비스 설명이 얕다면, 그 자체가 훌륭한 첫 문장 재료가 됩니다. 핵심은 추측을 덜고 사이트에서 바로 확인 가능한 표현을 쓰는 것입니다.
items: 페이지 위치와 요소를 구체적으로 언급하기
과장 없이 가능한 영향만 설명하기
서비스 오퍼와 연결되는 각도만 남기기

example.title: 콜드메일 각도 예시
example.copy: 제품 설명은 명확한데 데모 CTA가 첫 스크롤 뒤에 있어, 이미 관심 있는 방문자도 한 번 더 찾아야 할 수 있습니다.
items: 첫 문장: 구체적이고 검증 가능함
각도: 전환 의도 손실 가능성
다음 단계: 첫 화면 CTA 아이디어 3개 제안

faqs.question: 각도는 항상 사이트 문제여야 하나요?
faqs.answer: 꼭 그렇진 않습니다. 강점도 각도가 될 수 있지만, 보통은 전환 마찰이나 정보 공백처럼 행동에 연결되는 신호가 더 유용합니다.

faqs.question: AI가 각도를 써도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 가능하지만, 최종 발송 전에는 반드시 원본 사이트 근거와 문장이 일치하는지 확인해야 합니다.

ko.title: 아웃리치 전에 잠재고객 웹사이트 점수 매기는 방법
ko.description: 적합도, 사이트 신호, 신뢰도, 연락 경로를 기준으로 잠재고객 웹사이트를 평가하는 가이드입니다.
ko.intent: 웹사이트를 저장하거나 아웃리치하기 전에 어떤 기준으로 점수를 매길지 설명합니다.
heroBullets: ICP 적합도와 웹사이트 신호를 함께 봅니다.
감으로 저장하지 않고 일관된 기준으로 선별합니다.
저장 기준을 개인 사용자 전체가 공유할 수 있게 만듭니다.

sections.title: 점수 모델에 꼭 들어가야 할 항목
sections.copy: 사이트 점수는 예쁘게 보이는지 평가하는 것이 아닙니다. ICP 적합도, 사이트에서 보이는 신호, 신뢰도, 연락 경로 준비도를 함께 봐야 실제 영업 우선순위에 쓸 수 있습니다.
items: ICP와 오퍼 적합도
전환·신뢰·콘텐츠 같은 사이트 신호
연락 경로와 내보내기 준비도

sections.title: 저장 기준선을 만드는 방법
sections.copy: 점수 모델의 목적은 숫자를 만드는 것이 아니라 저장 여부를 빠르게 판단하는 데 있습니다. 적합도는 높지만 신호가 약한 계정과, 신호는 많지만 ICP가 맞지 않는 계정을 분리해야 합니다.
items: 저장 최소 점수선 정하기
근거가 2개 이상 있는 계정 우선
첫 문장까지 나오는 계정을 고우선순위로 보기

example.title: 간단한 점수 규칙 예시
example.copy: ICP 적합 40점, 사이트 신호 35점, 신뢰도 15점, 연락 경로 준비도 10점처럼 나누면 저장 기준을 정하기 쉽습니다.
items: 80점 이상 + 근거 2개 이상
첫 문장이 자연스럽게 나오면 저장
근거가 약하면 보류 또는 제외

faqs.question: 점수는 개인 사용자마다 달라도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 가중치는 달라질 수 있지만, 저장 기준과 필수 근거 수는 개인 사용자 안에서 통일하는 편이 좋습니다.

faqs.question: 점수만 높으면 바로 CSV으로 보내도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 점수는 시작점일 뿐입니다. 출처 메모와 첫 문장까지 검토한 뒤 내보내는 편이 안전합니다.

ko.title: 웹사이트 진단 영업 메일: 유용한 제안을 먼저 보내는 법
ko.description: 가벼운 웹사이트 진단을 실제 답장을 부르는 첫 아웃리치 메일로 바꾸는 방법을 설명합니다.
ko.intent: 웹사이트 진단 관찰을 활용해 더 유용하고 부담 없는 첫 영업 메일을 만드는 방법을 설명합니다.
heroBullets: 진단을 무료 컨설팅처럼 길게 보내지 않습니다.
가장 강한 관찰 한두 개만 골라 첫 메일에 씁니다.
회의 요청보다 답장하기 쉬운 다음 단계를 제안합니다.

sections.title: 진단형 아웃리치의 핵심
sections.copy: 웹사이트 진단형 메일은 '여기에 문제가 많습니다'가 아니라 '이 부분은 이렇게 개선할 수 있겠습니다'라는 톤에 가깝습니다. 첫 메일은 가볍게 시작해야 답장 장벽이 낮습니다.
items: 가장 강한 관찰 1~2개만 선택하기
관찰을 비즈니스 영향과 연결하기
짧은 제안이나 아이디어 전송을 다음 단계로 두기

sections.title: 첫 메일에 넣기 좋은 진단 포인트
sections.copy: 첫 화면 CTA, 사례 노출, 서비스 설명, 문의 경로, 콘텐츠 공백처럼 바로 확인 가능한 요소가 좋습니다. 이런 신호는 상대가 읽는 순간 사실 여부를 검증할 수 있기 때문입니다.
items: CTA와 히어로는 웹디자인·CRO 각도
사례와 증거는 신뢰·전환 각도
콘텐츠와 서비스 페이지는 SEO 각도

example.title: 진단형 영업 메일 예시
example.copy: 홈페이지 설명은 분명한데 사례 블록이 메인 CTA 아래에 있어, 이미 관심 있는 방문자도 데모 전에 충분한 신뢰 근거를 보지 못할 수 있습니다.
items: 관찰: 증거 노출이 늦음
각도: 예약 전 신뢰 정보 부족
다음 단계: 사례와 CTA 개선 아이디어 3개 제안

faqs.question: 이런 메일이 무료 컨설팅처럼 보이지 않나요?
faqs.answer: 범위를 작게 잡으면 괜찮습니다. 첫 메일에는 관찰 1개와 짧은 제안 정도면 충분합니다.

faqs.question: 모든 계정에 진단형 메일을 써도 되나요?
faqs.answer: 아니요. 사이트에서 보이는 신호가 있고 그 신호가 당신의 서비스와 직접 연결될 때만 쓰는 편이 좋습니다.

de.title: B2B Lead Recherche Tool: Websites vor der Akquise prüfen
de.seoTitle: B2B Lead Recherche Tool für Solo-Profis | LeadCue
de.description: Prüfen Sie Prospect-Websites, erkennen Sie belastbare Akquise-Anlässe und priorisieren Sie B2B Leads vor der Kaltakquise.
de.intent: Zeigt, wie Solo-Profis anhand von Websites besser qualifizierte B2B Leads auswählen.
heroBullets: Starten Sie nicht mit einer generischen Leadliste, sondern mit der Website des potenziellen Kunden.
Prüfen Sie CTA, Belege, Inhalte, Positionierung und Kontaktwege, bevor ein Prospect in die Akquise geht.
Verwandeln Sie jede Beobachtung in einen Kaltakquise-Einstieg, einen Akquise-Anlass und eine CSV-Notiz.

sections.title: Was Website-basierte Lead Recherche bedeutet
sections.copy: Website-basierte Lead Recherche ist keine reine Datensammlung. Vor der Ansprache wird die öffentliche Website eines Unternehmens geprüft, um zu entscheiden, ob der Prospect zur eigenen Dienstleistung passt und ob es einen konkreten, belegbaren Grund für die Kontaktaufnahme gibt.
items: Geeignet für Webdesign-, SEO-, CRO-, Growth- und PositionierungsSolo-Profis.
Der Fokus liegt auf besseren Prospects, nicht auf mehr Namen in einer Liste.
Ein gutes Ergebnis erklärt, warum ein Prospect jetzt angesprochen werden sollte.

sections.title: Welche Website-Signale wichtig sind
sections.copy: Die stärksten Signale hängen mit geschäftlichen Ergebnissen zusammen. Ein versteckter CTA kann Demo-Anfragen kosten, fehlende Fallstudien können Vertrauen schwächen, und dünne Service-Seiten können ein guter Einstieg für SEO- oder Messaging-Angebote sein.
items: Above the fold: Headline, Nutzenversprechen, CTA und Trust-Elemente.
Belege: Cases, Testimonials, Logos, Ergebnisse und Branchenbezug.
Inhalte und Wege: Aktualität, Seitentiefe, Navigation und Kontaktpfad.

sections.title: Aus Belegen wird Akquise-Kontext
sections.copy: Eine gute Nachricht sagt nicht nur, dass Sie die Website gesehen haben. Sie verbindet eine konkrete Beobachtung mit einer möglichen Auswirkung und einem kleinen nächsten Schritt. So wirkt die Ansprache recherchiert statt automatisiert.
items: Der Einstieg nennt eine konkrete Beobachtung auf der Website.
Der nächste Satz verbindet diese Beobachtung mit Conversion, Vertrauen, SEO oder Kontaktpfad.
Der Call to Action bleibt klein, zum Beispiel drei kurze Verbesserungsideen.

example.title: Beispiel für eine Lead-Recherche-Notiz
example.copy: Northstar Analytics erklärt das Produkt klar, aber der Demo-CTA und der Beleg für Finance-Solo operators erscheinen erst nach dem ersten Scrollen.
items: Signal: Der Demo-Pfad ist im ersten View nicht sichtbar.
Akquise-Anlass: Besucher können das Produkt verstehen, ohne sofort einen klaren nächsten Schritt zu sehen.
Nächster Schritt: Drei Ideen für Hero-CTA, Proof-Block und Case-Study-Pfad senden.

faqs.question: Ist Website-basierte Lead Recherche dasselbe wie Scraping?
faqs.answer: Nein. Es geht um sichtbare öffentliche Website-Informationen, menschliche Bewertung und belegbaren Akquise-Kontext, nicht um das massenhafte Sammeln von Kontaktdaten.

faqs.question: Für welche Solo operators ist das geeignet?
faqs.answer: Besonders für Webdesign-, SEO-, CRO-, Growth- und Messaging-Solo-Profis, weil ihre Angebote direkt mit sichtbaren Website-Signalen verbunden werden können.

faqs.question: Was sollte ins CSV übernommen werden?
faqs.answer: Firma, URL, Fit-Score, wichtigste Website-Signale, Quellen-Notizen, E-Mail-Einstieg, Status, Stage und Exportstatus.

de.title: AI website prospecting tool: qualify prospects before outreach
de.seoTitle: AI Website Prospecting Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
de.description: Use AI website prospecting to find opportunity signals, score prospect fit, and prepare source-backed first lines before outreach.
de.intent: Define the LeadCue method for solo professionals who research prospect websites before outreach.
heroBullets: Start with what the prospect's website already proves instead of a generic contact record.
Use opportunity signals and fit scoring to decide whether the prospect deserves outreach.
Turn the research into a Prospect Card that carries context into CSV or email workflows.

sections.title: What AI website prospecting changes
sections.copy: Traditional outbound often starts with a list and searches for a contact. AI website prospecting starts with the company website and asks whether there is a credible reason to contact the prospect at all.
items: The first decision is qualification, not email discovery.
The research output is a source-backed reason to contact, not a vague personalization line.
The handoff keeps website evidence, fit score, first line, and export fields together.

sections.title: Workflow matrix
sections.copy: The workflow should move from public evidence to prospect judgment to outreach preparation without losing context between tools.
items: Review: inspect homepage, proof, CTA, service pages, content freshness, and contact path.
Qualify: score ICP fit, signal strength, confidence, and outreach readiness.
Prepare: save a Prospect Card with source notes, first line, outreach angle, follow-up status, and export status.

sections.title: When it is the right motion
sections.copy: Website prospecting is strongest when your offer can be connected to visible website improvements or missed opportunities.
items: Web design freelancers can use CTA, proof, navigation, and conversion signals.
SEO consultants can use thin pages, stale content, weak internal paths, and missing intent pages.
Growth consultants can use positioning, campaign path, lead magnet, and proof gaps.

example.title: Website-first research example
example.copy: Instead of saving a SaaS company because it matches an industry filter, you save it because the site has a clear product story, hidden finance proof, and no above-the-fold demo CTA.
items: Signal: proof and CTA are not visible when intent is highest.
Fit: your offer is conversion-focused website improvements.
Output: a Prospect Card with a verifiable first line and export-ready note.

faqs.question: Is AI website prospecting the same as scraping?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow uses visible public website evidence, human review, and source-backed notes. It is not designed to scrape social platforms or build hidden contact intelligence.

faqs.question: Who should use AI website prospecting?
faqs.answer: It is strongest for SEO consultants, web design freelancers, CRO specialists, growth consultants, positioning consultants, and solo founders because their offers can be tied to website evidence.

faqs.question: What should be produced before outreach starts?
faqs.answer: A saved Prospect Card should include fit score, opportunity signals, source notes, first line, reason to contact, and export status.

de.title: AI prospect research tool: the step before cold email
de.seoTitle: AI Prospect Research Tool for Solo Professionals | LeadCue
de.description: Learn why solo professionals need a prospect research tool that qualifies companies, preserves source evidence, and prepares outreach context before sending cold email.
de.intent: Position LeadCue as the research layer between prospect lists and outreach execution.
heroBullets: A spreadsheet stores prospects after they are accepted; a research layer decides whether they should be accepted.
An email tool sends messages; a research layer prepares the source-backed reason to send one.
A website research layer keeps Prospect Cards, fit scores, and opportunity signals together.

sections.title: Where the research layer sits
sections.copy: Many solo professionals have contact sources, spreadsheets, and email tools, but no dedicated place to decide whether a website-backed prospect is worth outreach.
items: Before export: qualify the website and avoid adding weak prospects.
Before email: prepare first lines and context that can be verified.
Before follow-up: preserve why the prospect was selected, skipped, or reviewed.

sections.title: Decision table for tool boundaries
sections.copy: A focused research layer should not pretend to replace every outbound tool. Its value is to improve the quality of prospects and context handed downstream.
items: Contact database: finds people or emails; research layer explains why the company is worth contacting.
Pipeline tool: manages follow-up status; research layer creates the source-backed context before export.
Email sequencer: sends campaigns; research layer prepares claims, first lines, and notes before sending.

sections.title: Signals the layer should preserve
sections.copy: The layer becomes useful when every saved prospect carries enough context for you to understand the decision later without repeating the research.
items: Website evidence: page observation, source path, and buyer implication.
Qualification: ICP fit, confidence, save threshold, and opportunity category.
Handoff: first line, short email context, export fields, status, and follow-up notes.

example.title: Research layer handoff example
example.copy: You review a website, save a Prospect Card, and export only the qualified prospect with fit score, source notes, first line, and a short outreach angle attached.
items: The exported row receives context, not just a URL.
The email writer can verify the claim before sending.
You can review why the prospect was selected.

faqs.question: Does a website research layer replace an outreach tool?
faqs.answer: No. It sits before your outreach workflow and prepares qualified, source-backed prospect context for export or handoff.

faqs.question: Does it replace an email finder?
faqs.answer: No. Email finders help locate contacts. A website research layer helps explain whether the prospect is worth contacting and what the outreach should reference.

faqs.question: What makes the layer useful for solo professionals?
faqs.answer: Solo professionals can connect their offer to visible website opportunities, then save that evidence as a reusable Prospect Card.

de.title: Website opportunity signals solo professionals can use before outreach
de.seoTitle: Website Opportunity Signals for Solo Outreach | LeadCue
de.description: Use website opportunity signals to spot CTA, proof, SEO, content, positioning, and timing cues that can support source-backed personal outreach.
de.intent: Give solo professionals a signal taxonomy for turning website observations into outreach reasons.
heroBullets: Opportunity signals connect a visible page observation to a business-relevant outreach angle.
The best signals are specific enough for a buyer to verify and relevant enough for your offer.
Weak signals should trigger review or skip decisions instead of automatic export.

sections.title: Signal taxonomy
sections.copy: A useful taxonomy keeps you from treating subjective design opinions as sales evidence. Each signal should map to a buyer outcome and your offer.
items: Conversion signals: hidden CTA, unclear next step, confusing navigation, weak contact path.
Trust signals: missing case studies, buried proof, no results, weak testimonials, unclear customer segments.
Content signals: stale blog, thin service pages, missing use cases, weak internal linking, outdated resources.

sections.title: How to score signal strength
sections.copy: Signal strength depends on visibility, relevance, and confidence. A visible issue that matches your offer is stronger than a broad guess about the business.
items: Strong: the source is visible, the implication is clear, and the offer can help.
Medium: the pattern is plausible but needs another source or manual review.
Weak: the note is subjective, generic, or disconnected from your offer.

sections.title: Turn signals into outreach context
sections.copy: Signals become useful when they are converted into a reason to contact, a first line, and a Prospect Card note you can verify later.
items: Observation: what the page shows and where it appears.
Implication: why it may affect conversion, trust, discovery, or buyer confidence.
Next step: a low-pressure suggestion such as sending three ideas or a short teardown.

example.title: Opportunity signal example
example.copy: The product page is clear, but the strongest customer proof appears below the main CTA and the navigation does not surface industry-specific examples.
items: Category: trust proof and conversion path.
Reason to contact: buyers may need proof earlier before booking.
First line: mention the proof placement and offer a small set of ideas.

faqs.question: How many opportunity signals should a prospect need?
faqs.answer: One strong signal can be enough for a first touch. Two or three source-backed signals make qualification more reliable.

faqs.question: Are opportunity signals the same as a full audit?
faqs.answer: No. They are lightweight sales research cues used before outreach. A full audit can happen after the prospect engages.

faqs.question: What should happen when the signal is weak?
faqs.answer: Do not export the prospect automatically. Mark it for review, scan another page, or skip until a stronger reason to contact appears.

de.title: How to find a reason to contact a prospect from their website
de.seoTitle: Find a Reason to Contact Prospects from Website Evidence | LeadCue
de.description: Find a credible reason to contact a prospect by reviewing website signals, qualifying fit, and turning source evidence into a useful first line.
de.intent: Help solo operators solve the core problem of why this prospect should be contacted now.
heroBullets: A good reason to contact is specific, visible, relevant to the offer, and easy to verify.
The website should provide evidence, not just a name to personalize around.
If there is no credible reason, the prospect should be skipped or reviewed instead of exported.

sections.title: A simple qualification order
sections.copy: Start with prospect fit, then inspect the website for evidence, then decide whether the observation supports a useful first touch.
items: Fit: does the company match your ICP, offer, and target market?
Evidence: does the website show a specific opportunity signal or gap?
Message: can the observation become a first line and next step without overclaiming?

sections.title: Reason-to-contact checklist
